title: Confirmation: log sampling protocol and customer-data exposure clearance
preview:
From: Elena Morales <elena@finblue.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: quick confirmation on log sampling results

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the update over the phone this morning. The security team here needs a concise written confirmation that Redwood's log sampling and hash reconciliation for the 2026-11-23 incident did not show any access to FinBlue customer records.

Can you include:
- Short description of the sampling method used
- The time window examined
- Whether any customer identifiers or PII were present in the sample
- Any attachments or output we can use for our audit (hash manifest or sample csv)

We are aiming to close our internal audit ticket by COB tomorrow and this statement will suffice for now. If you need to loop in an independent reviewer, let us know.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Security Operations
FinBlue

---
Elena Morales
FinBlue | elena@finblue.com
Phone: +1 415 555 0134

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@finblue.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quick confirmation on log sampling results

Hi Elena — thanks for the call.

Short answer up front: our sampling and hash-reconciliation results show no evidence of FinBlue customer records being accessed or exfiltrated during the incident window. See details below and attachments.

What we examined
- Time window: 2026-11-23T02:15:00Z through 2026-11-23T03:40:00Z (system UTC times).
- Sources: API request logs, per-region access traces, and ephemeral inference cache purge logs across the affected host pool.
- Sample methodology: randomized stratified sampling across hosts and request IDs, plus deterministic hash-matching for known FinBlue customer identifiers (customer_id hashes supplied by FinBlue team).

Results summary
- Sample size: 5,400 log lines sampled (see finblue_log_sample.csv).
- Hash reconciliation: all known FinBlue customer identifier hashes either did not appear or only appeared in noise-level artifacts (internal test accounts) and were validated against the customer-provided list. Detailed hash list in hash_manifest.txt.
- No outgoing transfers or anomalous large-batch exports observed.

Attachments included: finblue_log_sample.csv (CSV), hash_manifest.txt (hashes and reconciliation notes), methodology_summary.pdf (sampling protocol and scripts used).

Next steps
- We can provide an independent checksum of the samples or an auditor-facing read-only Drive folder if required.
- If FinBlue needs a signed attestation on company letterhead, we can route that through legal.

Cc'ing Rishi and Marissa to add technical context if you want deeper artifact review.

Regards,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
+1 650 555 0210

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@finblue.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:28:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quick confirmation on log sampling results

Elena — adding a technical note on the exact verification we ran. Forwarding summary to make the audit trail explicit.

On Tue, Nov 24, 2026 at 10:03 AM Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena — thanks for the call.
> 
> Short answer up front: our sampling and hash-reconciliation results show no evidence of FinBlue customer records being accessed or exfiltrated during the incident window. See details below and attachments.

Technical verification steps (abbreviated)
1) Sample selection:
   - Deterministic stratified sample: 5% from each host in the affected pool, plus additional sampling on error-code endpoints.
   - Total sampled lines: 5,400; sample file attached.
2) Hash reconciliation:
   - We received HMAC-SHA256 hashes for customer_id and device_id from FinBlue (salted per your guidance).
   - We ran one-way checks (no reversal) against the sampled logs. All matches were cross-referenced to your test account list.
3) KV cache verification:
   - KV cache eviction windows were examined; no persistent entries containing customer_id hashes were present post-incident.
4) Egress audit:
   - No successful S3 PUTs, external transfers, or large outgoing streams tied to the affected instances in the window.

Conclusion: No sampled evidence of customer-record access or data exfiltration. We consider this a low-likelihood outcome given the coverage and methodology, but if you need higher confidence we can:
- Increase sampling coverage to 20% per host; or
- Provide a signed attestation and the underlying artifacts to your third-party auditor (read-only access).

Attachments referenced: finblue_log_sample.csv, hash_manifest.txt.

—
Rishi Malhotra
Staff SRE, Runtime Safety
rishi@redwood.com

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@finblue.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Audra Compliance <audit@securecheck.co>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 17:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quick confirmation on log sampling results

Elena,

Following up with a formal, customer-facing confirmation you can use for your internal audit ticket. This summarizes what Redwood ran and the resulting conclusion.

Formal statement (summary):
- Incident window examined: 2026-11-23T02:15:00Z — 2026-11-23T03:40:00Z.
- Artifacts reviewed: API access logs, host-level access traces, KV cache sweeps, and egress logs for the affected host pool.
- Methodology: randomized stratified sampling across hosts, deterministic HMAC-SHA256 reconciliation against FinBlue-provided salted hashes, and egress/transfer checks. Details in methodology_summary.pdf.
- Outcome: No evidence was found indicating that FinBlue customer records were accessed, written to external destinations, or exfiltrated as a result of the 2026-11-23 incident. Sample artifacts and hash manifest are attached.

If you would like a short attestation letter on Redwood letterhead, include the recipient and any specific phrasing required by your compliance team and we will escalate to Legal. We can also provide auditor access to the Drive folder linked here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/finblue-forensic-20261124

Regards,

Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
Phone: +1 650 555 0210

Attachments:
- finblue_log_sample.csv (CSV) — stripped and redacted sample lines used for verification
- hash_manifest.txt (TXT) — list of salted hashes used for reconciliation and notes on matches
- methodology_summary.pdf (PDF) — sampling protocol, scripts run, and reproducibility notes

title: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>, Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Hi Kara and Tom,

Quick kickoff for the Acme exec day on Dec 7 — two priority items I want to lock before vendor confirmations:

1) Guest flow and mic coverage: Acme wants unobtrusive table mics in the boardroom and a plex mic grid in the foyer for registration announcements. Attached is the latest floorplan (acme_floorplan_v3.pdf) and a proposed mic_grid_v2.png. Can you confirm:
- recommended number of lavs vs table mics for the 28-person boardroom
- power/snake runs we should reserve on load-in

2) Bridge fallback and SLA: Acme requires a redundant conferencing bridge with a warm failover (15s switchover) and call-recording retained for 30 days. Tom — can you share the SOW/quote for the fallback bridge and the exact latency we should expect?

Timing: we need vendor confirmation by EOD Wednesday (Nov 24) so procurement can start.

Attachments: acme_floorplan_v3.pdf, mic_grid_v2.png

Thanks,

Alex Martinez
Event Ops Lead, Customer Events
Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0143



From: Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Alex — thanks for the clear brief. High level answers below; will follow up with a short SOW PDF and quote by Monday.

- Boardroom mic guidance: For a 28-person boardroom with a single presenter setup + Q&A, we recommend:
  * 6 cardioid boundary table mics (distributed every 4–5 seats) for best pickup and minimal cabling
  * 2 lavs for presenter + backup
  * One ambient pair for room audio feed into the bridge
  Power: we need two clean AC drops at the head table and one distro on the east wall — snake runs noted on your floorplan are sufficient.

- Foyer/registration: a compact plex mic grid (4 mics) will cover announcements and will route to a front-of-house mixer. We'll provide a small DSP for gating.

- Bridge fallback: We can deliver a geo-redundant bridge with warm failover (target 10–12s for SIP/TLS sessions). Recording retention 30 days available as an add-on; will include a redundant egress path and monitoring.

Attachment: preliminary_quote_bridge_fallback.pdf (I'll send detailed SOW Monday).

Tom Rivera
Solutions Architect
AVBridge Co.
Office: +1 (206) 555-2233


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Thanks Tom — that lineup looks good. A couple of constraints from our side to keep in the SOW:

- Network: the boardroom VLAN is isolated (10.42.11.0/24). We need a list of IPs/services your bridge endpoints will call out to so our NetOps can pre-approve firewall rules.
- Latency and monitoring: Acme wants RTT < 120ms for trans-pacific calls; please include synthetic monitoring and alerting in the quote (pager escalation to our on-call if failover occurs).
- Recording storage: ensure recordings are encrypted at rest and accessible via a secure link for 30 days (audit logs required).

Also, Lauren — can you confirm room staging window (we requested 07:00–09:30 for load-in) and whether we have concierge to direct carriers to the loading dock?

Marcus Lin
Head of Site Reliability, Redwood Inference



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Jason Chen <jason@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Forwarding the notes and a slightly updated floorplan — small pod added near the east foyer for guest check-in (see acme_floorplan_v3.pdf).

Jason — looping you in to confirm guest arrival flow and whether two concierge staff are available to manage registration between 07:30–09:30. If Acme can provide badge lists via the shared Drive link, we'll pre-print badges and reserve the east doorway for a covered canopy (weather contingency).

Tom / Kara — two asks for Monday:
- Final SOW and line-item quote for mic grid + bridge fallback (include monitoring and encryption details)
- A terse cable run diagram showing power drops and snake runs (PDF) we can hand to the venue team at load-in

Attachments: acme_floorplan_v3.pdf (updated), mic_grid_v2.png

Best,
Alex Martinez
Event Ops Lead, Customer Events
Redwood Inference


From: Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Jason Chen <jason@acmecorp.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 15:36:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Alex / team — confirmable. Logistics notes below and a small attachment (bridge_fallback_SOW.pdf) with line items and lead times:

- Loading dock: we'll reserve a 90-minute window starting 07:00 for AV carrier load-in; two concierge staff assigned (Sarah P. and Omar T.) for guest routing and badge pickup 07:30–09:30.
- Canopy: east doorway reserved; vendor to provide 1x 20amp circuit for canopy heaters if needed.
- Cable runs: venue will permit floor snakes up to 30ft free of charge; anything longer billed per-foot (see SOW).
- Contact: On the day, site tech is Jonah L. (onsite 06:30–18:00) — he'll meet AVBridge and Acme rep at dock.

We included the mic grid placement and power drop labels in the attached PDF. Let me know if you want us to pre-stag a small backup bridge (on-site appliance) for 2 hours right before the event — we can quote that as an add-on.

Attachment: bridge_fallback_SOW.pdf

Thanks,
Kara Simmons
Senior Venue Liaison
Starlight Venues
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0786

title: Dependent care: part-time return schedule for Priya Natarajan
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Dependent care: proposed part-time RTW for Priya

title: Contract roll-forward: EU edge node + APAC warm-standby onboarding
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Kate Reynolds <kate@curavista.com>, Tom Liu <tom.liu@curavista.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Contract roll-forward: EU edge node + APAC warm-standby onboarding

Hi Kate / Tom —

Thanks again for the productive renewal discussion last week. Attached is a roll-forward proposal that keeps your existing SLA and adds two items we agreed to explore before signing:

- Dedicated EU edge node (single-tenant) to satisfy data residency and audit requirements
- APAC warm-standby capacity (reserved burst pool + automated failover) for planned expansion next quarter

High-level notes:
- Pricing is shown as two separable line items in the attached pricing workbook so you can accept the EU node now and stagger APAC capacity later.
- We propose a 12-month term roll-forward starting 2027-01-01 with a 30% discount on the APAC warm-standby for the first 6 months as an onboarding credit.
- Technical runbook and ownership: Soojin will be the primary technical lead; Marissa will manage onboarding milestones and success metrics.

Could you confirm the residency controls you require for the EU edge (specific country or region)? Also, Tom — can you share any procurement timing constraints? If this looks good, I can loop in Legal to start a light amendment and a DocuSign flow.

Attachments: SOW_CuraVista_v3.pdf, pricing-dedicated-warmstandby.xlsx

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142


From: Kate Reynolds <kate@curavista.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Liu <tom.liu@curavista.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 13:45:12 -0000
Subject: Re: Contract roll-forward: EU edge node + APAC warm-standby onboarding

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the proposal and the clear split for EU vs APAC line items. A few clarifying questions from our side before we move to legal:  

1) Residency: We need EU data to remain in the EU (preferably Germany or Ireland). Is the edge node single-country pinning, or a multi-AZ within the EU? We also need documented evidence for our auditors (ISO/SOC).
2) Failover / RTO: The APAC warm-standby is intended for traffic surges only — what's the tested failover RTO and RPO for cross-region traffic? We need an SLA addendum if the standby becomes primary.
3) Cost: Can we see a blended comparison: current hosted vs. EU node + warm-standby expected monthly spend at 25% utilization? Your pricing sheet had nominal numbers but a blended example would help our CFO sign off.
4) Timeline: We aim to have EU residency in place before our Q2 2027 pilot in Europe. Can you commit to a target onboarding date?

Also, one more ask — we prefer the EU node to be provisioned under a separate subaccount so our data classification tooling can scope it easily. Is that supported?

Regards,
Kate Reynolds
Director of Platform Ops
CuraVista Health
kate@curavista.com


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:03:22 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Contract roll-forward: EU edge node + APAC warm-standby onboarding

Team —

Filing Kate's questions above. Can we get a compact deliverable set to send back to the customer by EOD Monday? Specifically:  

- Soojin: Short technical note that covers EU-country pinning options (Germany v Ireland), deployment model (single-tenant node vs logical tenancy), cross-region replication approach, and failover RTO/RPO with test cadence.
- Marissa: Blended cost example (current spend vs expected with EU node + warm-standby at 25% avg util) and proposed onboarding timeline to meet a Q2 2027 pilot. Also confirm subaccount provisioning workflow.
- Ben: Are there any commercial flexes we should pre-approve (e.g., extended onboarding credit, trial APAC burst free for 30 days)?

I'll incorporate the answers into a short amendment draft and confirm whether Legal needs to redline or if we can do a short SOW addendum.

Thanks —
Ava


From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:22:04 -0800
Subject: RE: Contract roll-forward: EU edge node + APAC warm-standby onboarding

Ava / team —

See quick tech note below to send to Kate:  

1) EU residency options
   - Country-pinned single-tenant node (recommended for auditors): provision dedicated node in Germany (Frankfurt) or Ireland (Dublin). Both support HSM-backed key management and dedicated logging.
   - Multi-AZ EU logical tenancy (lower cost): data stays in EU region but may not provide single-customer isolation required by some auditors.

2) Subaccount & data scoping
   - We can provision a customer subaccount under their organization with separate KMS keys and VPC peering. This meets their data classification requirement.

3) Cross-region warm-standby architecture
   - APAC warm-standby is a reserved pool configured with autonomous warming (KV cache priming + scheduled lightweight synchronization).
   - Failover model: automated DNS switch + state rehydration. Typical RTO in tests: 8-12 minutes for routing and cache rehydrate (dependent on cached prefix sizes). RPO: near-zero for persisted data; ephemeral cache may require rehydration.
   - If standby is promoted to primary for >72 hours, automated pricing fallback applies per the SOW (we'll include an SLA addendum for promotion events).

4) Test cadence and runbooks
   - We propose a staged test: dry-run (no traffic) -> controlled promotion (10% traffic) -> failover drill (full traffic). Each stage has a runbook and rollback plan; schedule ~2 weeks for validation in dev/stage, 1 week for production cutover.

5) Timeline (targeting Q2 pilot)
   - EU node provisioning: 6 weeks from signed amendment (procurement + infra + security review).
   - Warm-standby config: parallel 4 week setup after EU node; end-to-end validation 2 more weeks.

I'll attach the short architecture diagram (eu-node-architecture.png) and a one-page runbook we can include in the SOW.

Soojin Lee
Principal Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
soojin.lee@redwood.ai


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Kate Reynolds <kate@curavista.com>, Tom Liu <tom.liu@curavista.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract roll-forward: EU edge node + APAC warm-standby onboarding

Kate / Tom —

Thanks for the quick review and the helpful questions. We updated the SOW and pricing workbook to reflect:  

- EU single-tenant node (Germany default; Ireland option) with separate subaccount and KMS scoping.
- APAC warm-standby as a separable line item; first 30 days at no charge, then billed only when promoted or when average util >15% for the month.
- Failover SLA addendum that documents tested RTO (8-12 mins) and promotion pricing triggers.
- Blended cost sheet attached per Marissa's request (see pricing workbook).

Next steps:  
1) Please confirm preferred EU country (Germany or Ireland).
2) If approved, I'll initiate a short amendment + DocuSign flow (link below). We can sign the amendment independently of the main MSA amendment if you prefer a narrow legal review.

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/transaction/abc123-sign
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/7623345/DL-2026-0827-CV

If you want, we can set a 30-minute call tomorrow to walk through the runbook and timeline.

Appreciate it —
Ben Carter
VP, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0199

title: Priority slot request: Nimbus billing-residency feature
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-18T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Nimbus: ask for priority slot for billing-residency feature

Team — quick context: Nimbus (enterprise healthcare customer) finished a procurement review today and Procurement signaled they want our billing residency option (tenant-level billing data residency in us-east/Gov-like isolation) baked into the contract schedule. They want it live before 2027-02-01 as a contractual milestone.

Why this matters: Nimbus is at $2.1M ARR annualized for year 1 and has indicated a multi-year commitment if we can meet the residency milestone. They also flagged SOC2 logs and audit/export cadence as must-haves but those are separate asks.

What I need from Product/Eng: internal prioritization for a small, scoped residency feature (billing data segregation + export path + audit flag) to be scheduled into our January sprint cycle. They are willing to sign a partial prepay (6 months) if we can agree to the milestone.

Attachments: nimbus-MAP-v2.pdf (deal summary, obligations), sla-redlines-20261115.docx (procurement redlines)

Can we get a triage decision + rough ETA by EOD Friday 11/20? If yes, I will coordinate a short alignment call with Nimbus (Alex Grant, Laura Chen).

—Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

title: PrivGPU capacity safety-net & operational slack play
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@summitai.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: PrivGPU capacity safety-net & operational slack play

Jordan — looping in the ops team. Thanks for the design call last week. This thread is to lock down a practical safety-net (operational slack + graceful degradation) for SummitAI's private GPU pool so we can finalize the provisioning and the SOW. High-level asks: please confirm your expected steady-state throughput target and the characteristics of your daily bursts so we can size the warm pool and admission control.

What we believe from the demo and telemetry:
- Target sustained throughput: ~50k tokens/s aggregate (across tenants)
- Peak daily bursts: up to ~120k tokens/s for windows of up to 20–45 minutes (most days shorter)
- Latency SLO: P95 generation latency ≤ 150ms for short-form prompts; tail can be allowed to slip during extreme bursts

Primary design goals for this 'safety-net play':
1) Headroom margin that preserves SLOs during typical bursts (warm pool + cache warmup).
2) Fast, predictable fallbacks: soft-per-tenant quotas + degraded model variants (smaller quantized model) before hard throttling.
3) Network/egress caps and admission control so cross-tenant spikes don't exhaust NICs.

I've attached an initial capacity_calculation (capacity_calc.xlsx) and a rough network topology (network_map.png). Internal ops — Vanessa/Irene/Karthik: please propose a concrete headroom target (percent), example GPU counts, and an operational runbook stub we can give SummitAI for procurement.

Jordan — if you can confirm: (a) whether burst windows are daily or irregular, (b) max concurrent open sessions during burst, and (c) whether SummitAI requires strict egress caps or per-tenant bandwidth guarantees. That will change our buffering strategy.

Next milestone: we want to finalize numbers and runbook for the pilot by end of week so procurement can start.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0187

Attachments: capacity_calc.xlsx (sheet: baseline_vs_burst), network_map.png (topology sketch)


From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@summitai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: SummitAI Procurement <procurement@summitai.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 11:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrivGPU capacity safety-net & operational slack play

Ben — thanks, appreciate the clear framing. Quick answers:
- Bursts: mostly daily and correlated with batch ingestion from our mobile clients (06:30–08:00 PST and a smaller window 18:00–19:00). We also see occasional marketing-driven spikes that are less predictable.
- Max concurrent sessions during peak: approx 18k concurrent inference sessions (short-lived) with spikes in token throughput.
- Egress: procurement prefers soft egress caps per-tenant (billing/alerting) but SummitAI will want a hard top-line cap for contractual reasons.

Procurement note: we will need a short runbook describing expected failover behavior and what is considered a 'service degradation' vs 'outage' for the pilot contract. Also please include a summary of what the warm pool does and how long it takes to fully warm caches.

I can share a small traffic sample (anonymized) if that helps your sizing.

Jordan
Jordan Lee
Head of Platform, SummitAI
jordan.lee@summitai.com


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@summitai.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrivGPU capacity safety-net & operational slack play

Thanks — quick sizing and an operational safety-net proposal from ops side. I kept the math conservative given SummitAI's 50k sustained / 120k burst estimate. Summary below; see attached runbook_draft.md for runflow and thresholds.

Executive summary (recommended pilot config):
- Baseline (steady ~50k tokens/s): provision 8 H100-ish GPU slots in the private pool with 20% headroom for variance. Rationale: measured sustained token throughput per H100 at our mixed prompt distribution is ~6k–7k tokens/s when using the optimized runtime and batching; 8 GPUs -> ~48–56k tokens/s effective depending on batch efficiency.
- Warm pool for short bursts: 4 additional warm-gpu slots (pre-initialized model instances) to absorb short 20–45 minute bursts; combine with KV-prefix caching and prefix-warmup to avoid cold-cache penalties. Warm pool target time-to-ready: ~30–90 seconds depending on model size and quantization.
- Admission control and graceful degradation: implement per-tenant soft quotas (alerts at 70% of quota), soft-throttle (30% latency-preserving rate limit) and automatic fallback to a quantized variant (Q4/Q5) at 90% quota before hard rejection at 100% quota. This preserves availability while protecting overall pool health.
- Network & egress safety: reserve 30% NIC/headroom on the crossconnect for burst egress. Add per-tenant egress budget alerts and a hard cap at contract value.

Operational safety-net thresholds (initial):
- Pre-warm trigger: sustained ramp >25% over 60s -> spin up warm slot (1 per trigger), up to 4 slots.
- Cache warmup acknowledgment: target 30s for common prefixes (measured during pilot).
- Circuit-breaker: if aggregate latency p95 > 300ms for 2 consecutive 60s windows -> begin soft-degradation (fall back models) and start informing SummitAI with a status event.

Capacity numbers and tradeoffs:
- If SummitAI demands stricter P95 < 120ms we need to increase baseline GPUs to 10 (reduces effective utilization and adds 15–25% infra cost).
- If warm pool must cover the full peak (120k) instead of being a buffer, we'd need ~10 extra fast instances — higher cost and longer procurement lead time.

Next steps / asks:
1) Jordan: confirm daily burst windows frequency vs amplitude (rough % of days with long bursts). The traffic sample would help tune pre-warm triggers.
2) Karthik: approve the 30% egress/NIC buffer and the fallback-to-quantized-model policy so we can include it in the SOW.
3) Ben: I included runbook_draft.md with play-by-play for incident ops and the initial monitoring alerts we want to add to the console. Please share with SummitAI procurement.

Vanessa Ortiz
Principal Ops Engineer, Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com

Attachments: runbook_draft.md (pilot runflow, thresholds, escalation contacts)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@summitai.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrivGPU capacity safety-net & operational slack play

+1 from infra leadership. Approved to proceed with the pilot as described with the following mandates: 1) reserve 30% egress/NIC buffer, 2) admission-control + fallback-to-quantized-model as the first line of degradation, 3) include a daily warm-pool health check in the ops runbook and automated alerts to SummitAI.

Ben — you can share the runbook and the capacity spreadsheet with procurement. Vanessa/Irene — please prepare a short list of telemetry events we will surface to SummitAI (warmpool_count, prewarm_triggers, p95_latency_window, hard_throttle_events) and a pilot cutover checklist for Friday deploy.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Infrastructure, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

title: Post-call: MAP draft & next steps — legal / ops dependencies
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@finedge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2026 15:10:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: MAP draft & next steps — legal / ops dependencies

Hi Anna —

Thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and the MAP draft attached (MAP_v1.xlsx):

Summary of what we covered
- Goal: Replace legacy inference pipeline with Redwood Inference for recommendation scoring; initial scope = prod pilot for 3 models (online + batch).
- Success metrics: 50% reduction in p99 latency for real-time API, 30% TCO reduction token-level for batch scoring within six months.

Proposed Mutual Action Plan (high level) — milestones / owners / target dates
1) Pilot kickoff (bench + integration) — target: 2026-11-29 — Owner: FinEdge (Anna), Redwood (Ben)
2) Legal & Security signoff (DPA + SOC2 evidence review) — target: 2026-12-06 — Owner: FinEdge legal / FinEdge security, Redwood legal (Marissa)
3) Procurement & commercial terms (SOW, commit level) — target: 2026-12-10 — Owner: FinEdge procurement (procurement@finedge.com), Redwood sales ops (Rishi)
4) Pilot completion & success review — target: 2027-01-31 — Owner: FinEdge engineering, Redwood customer success

Dependencies we flagged:
- SOC2 evidence and data handling appendix (attached summary).
- Procurement needs: invoice terms, PO process, and decision on commit level (3-month pilot vs 12-month committed).
- Legal: standard DPA redlines — Anna, you mentioned your counsel will want a short review window.

Commercial / credit ask noted on call
- You requested we consider an introductory credit on first-year usage tied to a 12-month commit.
- Attachment: MAP_v1.xlsx includes an initial pricing line item and two commercial scenarios (pilot/no-commit vs 12-month commit with credits).

Actions / asks from you
- Please confirm the internal owners listed above and whether your procurement prefers net30 or net45 invoicing.
- Can we tentatively aim to finalize the MAP and SOW by 2026-12-10 so procurement/legal have blanket deliverables to review?

I copied Marissa (legal lead) and Rishi (sales ops) so they can weigh in on the DPA timing and the credit request. If it's useful, I can escalate a formal credit request to leadership tomorrow.

Attachments: MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), SOC2_certificate_summary.pdf (evidence summary)

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123


From: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@finedge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: FinEdge Procurement <procurement@finedge.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2026 17:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call: MAP draft & next steps — legal / ops dependencies

Ben —

Thanks for the detailed recap and the MAP draft. A few clarifications from our side after the internal sync:

- Owners: confirmed — I will own Pilot kickoff & engineering integration. Procurement (cc'd) will own commercial terms. Legal/Security will own DPA and SOC2 validation.
- Procurement asks: our standard is net45 on first-year vendors; we can discuss net30 for annual commitments but procurement will need an approved SOW and PO before billing.
- Timeline: procurement requested a slightly longer window — they prefer target commercial signoff by 2026-12-17 rather than 12/10. Is that workable?

Commercial ask (from our procurement lead)
- FinEdge is asking for a 20% introductory credit on first-year consumption tied to a 12-month commit, or alternatively a $50k platform credit if we sign a 12-month commitment within 30 days of contract execution. Procurement also asked for a performance milestone tying credits to the pilot success review.

I will route the MAP to our legal and procurement teams now. They may follow up for SOC2 detail and DPA redlines.

Thanks —
Anna
Anna Lopez | Head of Data Platforms
FinEdge
anna.lopez@finedge.com | +1 617-555-0199

My recommendation (based on comparable deals):
- Preferred: 12-month commit with a 10% usage credit + $35k platform credit.
- Contingency: Approve escalation to 15% + $50k credit if FinEdge agrees to a higher onboarding/commit milestone (signed SOW + PO within 30 days).

What I need from you:

- Rishi: pricing approval for 12-month commit at the proposed credit levels and final guidance on whether we can add a performance-gated credit clause in the SOW.

Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 09:05:00 -0800

- We can provide the SOC2 summary and our standard DPA within the timeframe. No major redlines expected from our side; if FinEdge proposes significant changes to data residency or audit rights we will escalate but that is unlikely for a pilot.

- Prepare an amended SOW for DocuSign (link below) and include the performance-gated credit language.

Redwood Inference

Hi Rachel,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the throughput numbers look promising for our inference-heavy pipelines. Procurement is asking for clarity on two items before we move to signature:

1) Uptime tiers & credit calculation: Skycore typically buys 99.9% commit for mission-critical paths. We need explicit credit slabs and an example calculation for a monthly outage scenario.

2) On-call surcharge: our legal team flagged the optional on-call rapid-response add-on (30 min dev response) — please confirm the monthly surcharge and whether it applies pro rata during trial months.

Hi Alyssa — thanks for the clear asks and the procurement form. I've sketched an appendix for the 99.9% commit and the optional on-call add-on; attaching it here for your CFO. Key points up front:

- Uptime tiers we recommend: 99.5% (standard), 99.9% (enterprise), 99.95% (premium). Credit slabs: 5% monthly credit for <99.9%, 10% for <99.5%, 20% for <99.0%. Example calculation included on page 2.

- Calculation note: credits are computed on monthly invoice net of usage credits; example: for a $50k invoice, 5% credit = $2,500. We include how incident windows are aggregated across the month.

- On-call surcharge: $6,500/month for 24/7 rapid-response (30-min acknowledgement, 2-hour mitigation SLA). During trial/prorated months we bill pro rata to the day. We can also offer a reduced onboarding month rate of $3,250 if you commit to a 12-month contract.

- Concessions: for deals >= $200k ARR we can offer a one-time onboarding credit equal to one month of the selected plan (applied at invoice after go-live).

I've attached: SI-Redwood-SLA-Appendix.pdf and Redwood-Support-Price-Grid.xlsx. If procurement wants a redline, Ben can handle the language tweaks to the appendix.

Next step: if that aligns, I can upload a redline-ready DocuSign and loop in your procurement contact.

Best,
Rachel Kim

title: Pilot handoff & success checklist — ClearView Health
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah.lin@clearview.health>, Mark Evans <mark.evans@clearview.health>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot handoff & success checklist — ClearView Health

Thanks again for the time today — great conversation. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can get the pilot (30-day evaluation) underway without surprises.

TL;DR:
- Goal: Validate extraction and summarization quality across outpatient referral notes (goal: 90th percentile F1 >= 0.82 on target prompt set).
- Scope: 2 clinical workflows, ~5k de‑identified note samples, context window up to 2,048 tokens.
- Kickoff target: week of Nov 30; cutover to test environment Dec 6.

Key pain points you mentioned:
- Inconsistent entity extraction for medications and dosage.
- Latency sensitive when running batch summarization for daily reports.
- Need for audit-friendly logs and retention policy for compliance.

Success metrics we'll use during pilot (proposed):
1) Extraction accuracy (per-entity F1) — target: >= 0.82 for meds/dosage.
2) Summarization ROUGE-L / human eval pass-rate >= 85%.
3) 95th percentile latency for single-document summary < 350 ms on dedicated instance.
4) Cost-per-1k-doc baseline and target improvement (we'll report token-level cost).

Owners & actions I propose (please confirm):
- ClearView: provide de-identified sample set (5k notes) + schema by Nov 25 — Point: Sarah Lin.
- Redwood (Ben): environment setup, SSO + service account, and network allowlist template by Nov 26.
- Redwood (Karthik): prompt set + eval harness, initial quality baseline within 3 business days of data ingestion.
- Camila (me): overall cadence, weekly syncs, and pilot acceptance checkpoint.

Access & security items to start (attachments included):
- pilot_scope.pdf (summary of scope & data handling commitments)
- network_access_template.txt (ports, IP ranges, SSO metadata we need)
- prompt_samples.json (starting prompt set for extraction + summarization)

Next steps: please reply with any corrections to owners/timeline and confirm the data delivery date (Nov 25). If that works I’ll schedule the kickoff for Nov 30 and loop in our Solutions Engineer for a 60-minute technical onboarding.

Thanks — looking forward to getting started.

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

[attachment: pilot_scope.pdf (summary, 2 pages)]
[attachment: prompt_samples.json (stub)]
[attachment: network_access_template.txt (stub)]

To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Mark Evans <mark.evans@clearview.health>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 11:04:00 -08:00

Appreciate the clear recap, Camila — this aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Data delivery: we can deliver an initial 2k de-identified notes export by Nov 25, full 5k by Dec 3 (operational constraints). Is starting with 2k sufficient for the baseline?
- Compliance: we need confirmation that logs and PII redaction steps meet our internal audit. Can Ben share a short checklist?
- Access: our security team requires SAML metadata and an allowlist doc. We’ll try to get the SSO vendor info to you by Monday (11/23).

Also, one ask: we'd like to include a small subset of raw PDFs (n=100) to simulate OCR edge cases. If Redwood can accept PDFs into the ingestion pipeline that would be helpful.

Mark and I are good with the proposed owners. Kickoff week of Nov 30 works if we can lock the environment by Nov 29.

—
Sarah Lin
Director, Clinical Informatics — ClearView Health
sarah.lin@clearview.health | +1 (617) 555-0199

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>

Subject: Pilot handoff & success checklist — ClearView Health

Thanks again for the time today — great conversation. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can get the pilot (30-day evaluation) underway without surprises.

TL;DR:
- Goal: Validate extraction and summarization quality across outpatient referral notes (goal: 90th percentile F1 >= 0.82 on target prompt set).
- Scope: 2 clinical workflows, ~5k de‑identified note samples, context window up to 2,048 tokens.
- Kickoff target: week of Nov 30; cutover to test environment Dec 6.

Key pain points you mentioned:
- Inconsistent entity extraction for medications and dosage.
- Latency sensitive when running batch summarization for daily reports.
- Need for audit-friendly logs and retention policy for compliance.

Success metrics we'll use during pilot (proposed):
1) Extraction accuracy (per-entity F1) — target: >= 0.82 for meds/dosage.
2) Summarization ROUGE-L / human eval pass-rate >= 85%.
3) 95th percentile latency for single-document summary < 350 ms on dedicated instance.
4) Cost-per-1k-doc baseline and target improvement (we'll report token-level cost).

Owners & actions I propose (please confirm):
- ClearView: provide de-identified sample set (5k notes) + schema by Nov 25 — Point: Sarah Lin.
- Redwood (Ben): environment setup, SSO + service account, and network allowlist template by Nov 26.
- Redwood (Karthik): prompt set + eval harness, initial quality baseline within 3 business days of data ingestion.
- Camila (me): overall cadence, weekly syncs, and pilot acceptance checkpoint.

Access & security items to start (attachments included):
- pilot_scope.pdf (summary of scope & data handling commitments)
- network_access_template.txt (ports, IP ranges, SSO metadata we need)
- prompt_samples.json (starting prompt set for extraction + summarization)

Next steps: please reply with any corrections to owners/timeline and confirm the data delivery date (Nov 25). If that works I’ll schedule the kickoff for Nov 30 and loop in our Solutions Engineer for a 60-minute technical onboarding.

Thanks — looking forward to getting started.

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: Aurora Health - request: extended audit log retention & scoped waiver
preview:
From: Sara Miles <sara.miles@aurorahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Aurora Legal <legal@aurorahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: extended audit log retention for SOC 2 + SIG evidence

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the demo yesterday. Aurora's compliance team reviewed the SIG and we have a follow-up ask: for our upcoming SOC 2 Type II audit (Q1 2027) we need detailed request/response logs retained for 18 months for the Redwood integration so our auditors can sample traffic across the prior year.

Specifically: can Redwood provide an exception to retain per-request audit logs (including timestamps, request IDs, model, and token counts) for 18 months instead of the default retention? We understand this may be outside standard policy and are open to a scoped addendum / cost recovery.

Also attaching the auditor's evidence checklist and a brief note from our legal team. Can you confirm feasibility, any operational caveats, and an estimate for cost or engineering effort?

Thanks —
Sara
Sara Miles
Sr. Security Program Manager, Aurora Health
---
Attachments: aurora_evidence_checklist.pdf (pdf, 120KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Miles <sara.miles@aurorahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: extended audit log retention for SOC 2 + SIG evidence

Sara — thanks for the clear ask and for sending the checklist. Looping in Karthik (security/compliance) and Evelyn (legal/product) to evaluate technical feasibility and commercial terms.

Quick notes: our default platform retention for per-request detailed logs is 90 days; aggregated telemetry is kept 12 months. We have supported customer-specific exceptions before (example: export snapshots + legal hold or tenant-level extended retention behind a paid add-on). We will get you an options matrix (retention-by-type, cost estimate, audit export process) by EOD Monday.

Action items for us:
- Karthik: confirm whether we can retain raw per-request logs 18 months, impact on storage/backup, and any changes to privacy/data residency posture.
- Evelyn: confirm if a simple addendum + invoice for storage/backups will suffice or if legal needs different language.

Sara — can you confirm whether the auditor requires live access to logs (read-only API) or whether periodic export snapshots (CSV/Parquet) are sufficient?

Best,
Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
---
Attachments: none

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sara Miles <sara.miles@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 08:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: extended audit log retention for SOC 2 + SIG evidence

Ben / Sara —

Technical assessment below. TL;DR: feasible with an addendum; tradeoffs and recommended approach listed.

Current state (summary):
- Default detailed request logs (request_id, timestamp, model, input_size, output_size, request/response metadata) are retained for 90 days in our hot storage.
- Aggregated telemetry (errors, latency histograms) retained 12 months in long-term analytics store.

Options to meet 18 months requirement:
1) Export snapshots: schedule monthly snapshots of detailed logs for Aurora's tenant to an encrypted S3 bucket (Aurora-owned or Redwood customer bucket). Low infra change; minimal cost; auditors can be given read-only access to the export location.
2) Tenant-level extended retention: keep detailed logs in Redwood hot/cold storage for 18 months. Requires storage budget, retention policy changes, and a scoped SLA for redaction rules. Higher cost, higher operational complexity.
3) Hybrid (recommended): maintain 90-day hot logs + monthly encrypted snapshot exports for months 3-18. Snapshots stored as compressed Parquet with an index for sampling. Minimizes query latency impact, supports auditor sampling, and keeps legal hold semantics intact.

Operational caveats and compliance notes:
- We must confirm PII/data classification with Aurora to ensure exports do not violate data-minimization commitments.
- Snapshots can include request/response metadata; if Aurora needs full response payloads we will need signed customer approval (and may require redaction support).
- Cost estimate (ballpark): export + storage ~$1,200/month for 18-month retention at expected Aurora throughput (we'll provide a refined quote).

I'll attach a proposed addendum template and a sample export file to this thread for review. If Aurora wants indexed read-only API access to snapshots, that's an additional engineering scope (~3-4 weeks).

Karthik
Director, Security & Infrastructure | Redwood Inference
---
Attachments: redwood-soc2-log-matrix.xlsx, retention_export_sample.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Miles <sara.miles@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 09:33:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: extended audit log retention for SOC 2 + SIG evidence

Sara — attaching a concise proposal and next steps based on Karthik's recommendation (hybrid). Proposed commercial model: one-time setup + monthly storage fee. Evelyn, can you confirm whether the proposed addendum language attached is sufficient for legal?

Proposed next steps for Aurora:
- Confirm snapshot retention window (we recommended 18 months).
- Confirm whether snapshots must include full response payloads.
- Approve the addendum (I can route via DocuSign).
- Decide whether Aurora prefers Redwood-managed storage or their own S3 bucket (we recommend Aurora S3 for direct auditor access).

I'll tentatively hold engineering capacity for Dec if Aurora wants API-queryable snapshots.

Best,
Ben
---
Attachments: aurora_policy_addendum_v1.pdf

From: Sara Miles <sara.miles@aurorahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Aurora Legal <legal@aurorahealth.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 15:48:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: extended audit log retention for SOC 2 + SIG evidence

Thanks Ben / Karthik / Evelyn — this is helpful and aligns with our expectations.

Aurora confirms:
- We accept the hybrid approach (90-day hot logs + monthly encrypted snapshots) for the 18-month window.
- Snapshots should include full request metadata and response headers; response payloads only if Redwood can provide redaction options — otherwise we will accept payloads for the auditor under a controlled access arrangement.
- Aurora will provide an S3 bucket for Redwood to deposit encrypted snapshots.
- Please proceed with the addendum and send a cost estimate.

We're targeting to have the addendum signed before 2026-12-15 so our auditors can proceed with evidence collection.

Sara
Sr. Security Program Manager, Aurora Health
---
Attachments: none

preview:
From: AWS Marketplace <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Marketplace listing preflight: branding, claims & support window

Hi Ben,

Thanks for starting the listing process for Redwood Inference. We're doing a short preflight to confirm a few items before we pull assets into the portal and publish the draft listing. Can you please confirm the following by EOD (48 hours)?

1) Branding & logos
   - We need a square and a horizontal PNG (300x300 and 1200x300) and the trademark statement you want shown in the listing.

2) Claims & product copy
   - You currently list \"sub-100ms avg latency for short prompts\" in some materials. We need the definitive, publi shable phrasing (no internal qualifiers) and the evidence source or benchmark report.

3) Support / SLA windows
   - Marketplace requires you to publish the support coverage that customers can expect. Options are: 24x7, Business hours, or Business hours + Critical incident coverage. Please confirm which to display and provide any escalation contact info.

4) Evidence / artifacts
   - Please attach a support matrix, SOC/Security artifacts (if available), and the latest brand guidelines.

I've attached the official marketplace requirements doc for reference. Once we have these items we can create the draft listing and run it through the compliance check.

Thanks,
Katie Nguyen

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>

Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 09:08:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot playbook: econ owner alignment, security checklist & champion talk tracks

Elena — great conversation earlier. Quick recap and proposed playbook so we can align internal stakeholders at Brightlane and arm you as champion. Please confirm/edit the bold items below.

Call summary (high level)
- Goal: 6-week pilot focused on payment reconciliation use case to reduce manual matching and lower dispute resolution time.
- Primary metrics: reduce manual reconciliation time by 45%, reduce disputes by 30%, measurable per-month cost savings target (~$45k/mo estimated).

Stakeholder map (from today's convo) — please confirm names/roles:
- Economic buyer: Ravi Shah, VP Finance (decision authority for pilot spend)
- Security lead: Leah Patel (security & infra)
- Legal / contracts: Sam Cho (commercial and data/privacy)
- Procurement: procurement@brightlane.com (SLA/pricing gates)
- Champion / internal sponsor: Elena Torres (product operations)

Decision criteria we heard from your team:
1) Accuracy on reconciliation match rate (target >95% on pilot datasets)
2) Observable latency and cost per transaction (unit economics)
3) Data residency & access controls (SOC2 evidence required)
4) Clear rollback/fallback plan for operations

Immediate next steps (proposed)
1) We will send: mutual action plan + ROI one-pager + SOC2 summary (attached). Please share the correct procurement contact for contracting turnaround and whether a SOW or PO will be required.
2) Technical deep-dive with Karthik (30 min) to validate data format and integration points — suggested slots Tue/Wed next week.
3) Elena — I'll include two short champion talk tracks below you can use when briefing Ravi and Leah.

Champion talk tracks (2 x 30s):
- For Ravi (econ buyer): “Pilot is a quick, measurable test to reduce manual reconciliation overhead. Based on our scoping, expected net savings ~ $45k/mo with low integration lift — we can structure the pilot so you only pay for the resources used during the test and get a clear ROI readout at 6 weeks.”
- For Leah (security): “Redwood operates under a SOC2-compliant runtime; we’ll provide the SOC2 summary and can run an on-site/recorded security review. Data does not leave your VPC for private deployments — we can scope encryption and access controls to match Brightlane policies.”

Attachments: Brightlane_Mutual_Action_Plan.xlsx (MVP scope + timeline), RI_ROI_one-pager.pdf (high-level dollarized benefits), SOC2-summary.pdf

If the stakeholder list is accurate, I’ll send the MAP and one-pager now and book the technical sync. Thanks for championing this — small asks and clear timeline will help get Ravi comfortable.

— Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Decision criteria we heard from your team:

3) Data residency & access controls (SOC2 evidence required)
4) Clear rollback/fallback plan for operations

Based on our scoping, expected net savings ~ $45k/mo with low integration lift — we can structure the pilot so you only pay for the resources used during the test and get a clear ROI readout at 6 weeks.”

title: Liability cap framing and order-form redline proposal
preview:
From: Samir Desai <samir@gamma-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Nina Lopez <nina@gamma-analytics.com>
Cc: legal@gamma-analytics.com
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Liability cap - initial redlines

Ben — thanks for the demo last week. Our legal team reviewed the MSA/order form and pushed back primarily on the limitation of liability and the consequential damage carve-outs. High level asks:

- Liability cap to be equal to the greater of (i) fees paid in prior 12 months or (ii) $1M; Gamma prefers a firm floor rather than a multiple.
- Consequential damages waiver needs a carve-out for claims arising from data breach and IP infringement.
- Carve-outs for gross negligence and willful misconduct to be explicit (no cap).

I've attached our redlines (Gamma_MSA_redline_v3.docx) and a short liability summary. Can you confirm Redwood's position and timeline? We are aiming to finish procurement by EoN next week.

—Samir
Head of Procurement, Gamma Analytics

Attachments: Gamma_MSA_redline_v3.docx (docx), Liability_Summary_Gamma.pdf (pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Desai <samir@gamma-analytics.com>
Cc: Nina Lopez <nina@gamma-analytics.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Liability cap - initial redlines

Samir — thanks for sending these over and for the clear summary. Appreciate the urgency. A couple of framing points from our side so we can make fast progress:

- Redwood's standard position is a liability cap tied to fees paid. We typically do a multiple (2–3x) of fees paid in the prior 12 months for aggregate exposure, with a hard ceiling for larger customers.
- We are comfortable carving out matters like bodily injury, wilful misconduct and indemnity claims from the cap. Data breach carve-outs are reasonable in principle but we push back on an uncapped exposure where there are no agreed service limits/controls.

Proposed quick compromise that might land with Gamma legal:
1) Liability cap = greater of (i) 3x fees paid in the prior 12 months, or (ii) $250,000; hard aggregate cap not to exceed $750,000.
2) Carve-outs: (a) claims resulting from Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct; (b) third-party indemnities (IP infringement); (c) breach of confidentiality where personal data is exposed.
3) Agree to a limited data breach remedy ladder (incident response support + credits + defined remediation costs) rather than open-ended consequential damages.

If you can share which figure (floor or ceiling) matters most to Gamma, we can move faster. I'd like to loop in legal early — Laura, can you weigh in on the compromise language and any mandatory redlines?

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter | Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Liability cap - initial redlines (Gamma) — need legal view

Laura / Karthik / Connor — forwarding Gamma's redline and my suggested compromise below. They want procurement closed next Friday. Key negotiation points: floor vs multiple, hard ceiling, and data-breach carve-out scope.

My suggested compromise to propose to Gamma (I drafted this for Samir):
- Aggregate liability cap = greater of (a) three times the fees paid by Gamma in the prior 12 months, or (b) $250k, provided that the aggregate cap shall not exceed $750k.
- Carve-outs: gross negligence/willful misconduct (no cap); IP indemnity (no cap); confidential data exposure where personal data is leaked (cap limited to direct remediation costs and regulatory fines limited to $X).
- Exclude consequential damages waiver for recovery of direct remediation costs tied to data incident.

Questions for legal: is the combined "3x prior 12 months with $750k ceiling" something you can defend? Is there specific language you'd want around incident-response remediation vs open-ended costs? Also advise on whether we can keep regulatory fines within cap or need to carve them out.

Attachments: OrderForm_Gamma_redline.xlsx, Gamma_MSA_redline_v3.docx

Thanks — Ben

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 13:17:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Liability cap - initial redlines (Gamma) — need legal view

Ben — read the redlines. Short answer: the compromise you proposed is defensible if we tighten some language and add a few operational guardrails. Specific guidance below (high level):

1) Cap mechanics: 3x prior 12-month fees with a $750k ceiling is reasonable commercial middle ground. We should replace the ambiguous \"fees paid\" phrase with a defined formula: \"Fees paid by Customer to Redwood under the Agreement during the 12-month period immediately preceding the event that gave rise to liability (as invoiced and not disputed).\" That prevents retroactive gamesmanship.

2) Floor vs absolute floor: they asked for a $1M floor — counter with a modest floor ($250k) plus the 3x multiplier. Keep aggregate cap hard.

3) Carve-outs: keep gross negligence and willful misconduct uncapped. For data breaches, we should carve out direct remediation costs and statutory regulatory fines from the cap only where the breach results from Redwood's proven gross negligence or willful misconduct; otherwise remediation costs are within cap. That balances risk and keeps some limit for good-faith incidents.

4) Consequential damages: preserve a broad consequential damages waiver but explicitly exclude the narrow categories of third-party IP indemnity and liabilities arising from Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct. For data incidents, provide an explicit remedies ladder (investigation, remediation, customer notification costs, and pre-agreed crediting policy) — this avoids open-ended consequential claims.

5) Practical asks we should request from Gamma in return: (a) a 12 month minimum committed term or minimum annual spend threshold if they want a higher floor; (b) timely cooperation on incident response and access to logs; (c) liability insurance evidence (existing coverage). These commercial asks help reduce our risk appetite.

I drafted redline language and attached it (Liability_Summary_Gamma.pdf has the short rationale; redline included in the MSA docx). Short timeline: if Ben gets back to them with the 3x + $750k ceiling + proposed operational guards, legal can support it for signature next week.

Laura Bennett
Associate General Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachments: Liability_Summary_Gamma.pdf (pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Desai <samir@gamma-analytics.com>

Subject: Re: Liability cap - initial redlines

Samir — thanks again and quick update. After internal review we can move to the following commercial compromise: 

1) Aggregate liability cap: the greater of (i) three times the fees paid by Gamma to Redwood in the prior 12 months (defined as invoiced and not disputed), or (ii) $250,000; provided that the aggregate cap shall not exceed $750,000.

2) Carve-outs (no cap): (a) claims resulting from Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct; (b) third-party IP indemnity obligations; (c) regulatory fines resulting from Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct.

3) Data incidents: where a data breach results from Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct, remediation costs and regulatory fines shall be excluded from the cap. For other incidents, we agree to an incident response/remediation ladder: (i) investigation and forensics, (ii) remediation work and reasonable third-party costs, (iii) customer crediting consistent with the Service Level Schedule — collectively recoverable within the aggregate cap.

4) Mutual commercial concessions we request: (a) 12-month minimum term on the Order Form or $100k minimum annual committed spend; (b) agreed cooperation and access for incident response; (c) evidence of insurance coverage (general liability + cyber) prior to signature.

Next steps: I'm attaching the Order Form redline and our marked liability language. If Gamma can confirm acceptance of the framework today we will issue a DocuSign link for the order form and have legal push the MSA redline for signature. Otherwise we can hop on a 30-minute call.

title: CryptoEdge: tailored data destruction playbook & SIG evidence
preview:
From: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Reeves <procurement@cryptoedge.io>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0500
Subject: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the product demo last week — CryptoEdge is excited about Redwood Private for our inference workloads. Before we move to a PO, the security team has two closely related asks we need to resolve: a tailored data destruction (proof-of-delete) playbook for tenant-scoped logs/artifacts, and the SIG/CAIQ evidence mappings that explain how that playbook meets standard control objectives.

Specifically we need:
- A documented playbook that describes the steps Redwood will take to delete tenant session logs, ephemeral KV cache snapshots, and any diagnostic dumps for a dedicated Private deployment (we run in a VPC with customer-managed KMS).
- A sample proof-of-delete export from a non-prod tenant that shows the notification manifest, deletion timestamps, and checksum evidence (PDF or CSV).
- SIG/CAIQ cells or attachments that tie those destruction steps to the relevant control IDs (data lifecycle, retention, and forensics).

To be clear: CryptoEdge requires the ability to demonstrate to our internal auditors that we can obtain customer-scoped proof-of-delete for logs older than 90 days and for ephemeral caches. We also want to confirm behavior when a legal hold is asserted (how deletion is paused and how evidence is preserved).

Attached is our SIG template (CryptoEdge_SIG_Request_20261121.pdf) — it highlights the cells we care about. Can your team confirm an expected deliverable list and an estimated timetable? Ideally we would like artifacts by next Wednesday (2026-11-24) if feasible.

Thanks,
Samantha Liu
Head of Security, CryptoEdge
Samantha.Liu@cryptoedge.io

--
Attachment: CryptoEdge_SIG_Request_20261121.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Mark Reeves <procurement@cryptoedge.io>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Samantha — thanks for the clear ask and the template. Looping in Priya (security lead for Private) and Neha (compliance/evidence) to scope deliverables and timeline.

Summary of what we will deliver (first pass):
1) A tailored "Tenant Destruction Playbook" describing steps for: identification of artifacts (logs, KV snapshots, diagnostic bundles), deletion orchestration, KMS key handling for objects, and manifest/checksum generation.
2) A sample proof-of-delete export from a staging tenant: CSV manifest + generated SHA256 checksums + deletion timestamps.
3) SIG/CAIQ mappings (we will populate the CryptoEdge template you attached) and callouts to SOC 2 procedures where relevant.

Clarifying questions so we scope correctly:
- Are you asking for proof-of-delete for all tenants globally, or only for the dedicated tenant we showed? (staging vs. production)
- Do you currently have any active legal holds or investigation flags that would prevent deletion for historical data?

Target timeline: Priya/Neha — can you confirm whether a 3-business-day turnaround for items (1)-(3) is realistic from your end? If not, we should propose a short meeting to prioritize cells in the SIG template.

I attached our working SIG responses workbook (redwood_sig_responses_v1.xlsx) — this is preliminary and will be updated.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

--
Attachment: redwood_sig_responses_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 09:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Thanks for the context, Samantha — and thanks Ben for looping us in. Quick runbook / proposed approach below; I copied the parts we will need from your template into action items.

High-level approach (what we'll produce):
- Playbook doc (PDF + editable Google Doc): scope, roles/responsibilities, triggers for deletion (age-based, customer request, on-termination), legal-hold overrides, verification steps, and recovery/retention exceptions. Will include specific steps for tenant-scoped Redwood Private artifacts (session logs, KV cache snapshots, model-serving debug bundles).
- Proof-of-delete export: a structured manifest (CSV) that contains object id, object type, tenant id, generated checksum, deletion timestamp, deletion operator id, and a signed notice (HMAC) produced by our evidence pipeline.
- SIG/CAIQ mapping: populate the CryptoEdge template and annotate with our SOC 2 procedure references and sample artifacts.

Technical clarifications needed from CryptoEdge:
1) Tenant identifiers: please provide the tenant id(s) you want included in the sample export (staging tenant vs. prod).
2) Legal hold process: confirm who (email) would assert a hold and the expected timeframe to lift it — we need to include hold-handling in the playbook.
3) Export format preference (CSV + ZIP of manifests, or PDF summary + CSV) — we can produce both, but staging export size/time differ.

Notes on timeline and constraints:
- We can produce a draft playbook and populated SIG cells within 3 business days if CryptoEdge can provide tenant ids and confirm there are no active legal holds.
- The proof-of-delete export will be generated from our staging cluster; because of sampling limitations we will provide a mini-export that is representative (not exhaustive), plus instructions for how a full export would be generated in prod.
- For KMS-managed objects we will include the KMS key metadata and demonstrate how key-cycling is handled, but final key-rotation artifacts will require coordination with your KMS team.

Proposed next step: 30-minute sync to confirm tenant ids and legal hold procedures. I have availability Wednesday 11/24 10:00-10:30 PT or Thursday 11/25 09:00-09:30 PT. Let me know what works and we'll send a calendar invite.

- SIG/CAIQ mapping: populate the CryptoEdge template and annotate with our SOC 2 procedure references and sample artifacts.

Technical clarifications needed from CryptoEdge:
1) Tenant identifiers: please provide the tenant id(s) you want included in the sample export (staging tenant vs. prod).
2) Legal hold process: confirm who (email) would assert a hold and the expected timeframe to lift it — we need to include hold-handling in the playbook.
3) Export format preference (CSV + ZIP of manifests, or PDF summary + CSV) — we can produce both, but staging export size/time differ.

Notes on timeline and constraints:
- We can produce a draft playbook and populated SIG cells within 3 business days if CryptoEdge can provide tenant ids and confirm there are no active legal holds.
- The proof-of-delete export will be generated from our staging cluster; because of sampling limitations we will provide a mini-export that is representative (not exhaustive), plus instructions for how a full export would be generated in prod.
- For KMS-managed objects we will include the KMS key metadata and demonstrate how key-cycling is handled, but final key-rotation artifacts will require coordination with your KMS team.

title: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Hi Emily — great to connect earlier. Quick recap from our end and a concrete ask so your procurement and engineering teams can align: 

Summary of what we heard on the call:
- Pilot scope: ingest + realtime summarization for patient intake (avg request 1.8k tokens, 95th pct 5k).
- Expected concurrency: 6–12 RPS at peak daytime hours.
- Desired pilot length: 6 weeks, with a strong chance to expand to production if latency and accuracy meet SLAs.

Cost/volume hypothesis (ballpark):
- If you run ~10k requests/day at an average 1.8k tokens/request -> ~18M tokens/day -> ~126M tokens/week.
- Redwood target unit econ for this workload (inferred model mix): $32 / 1M tokens (mixed quantized model + caching).
- That maps to ~$4,032/week (~$16k/month) nominal inference cost at 126M tokens/week.

Ask and recommendation:
1) For a low-friction pilot, would Horizon accept a $15k credit top-up to cover the first 6 weeks of heavy experimentation? That would give you ~468M tokens of runway at the $32/1M assumption.
2) If your procurement needs a different framing, we can convert the ask to a 90-day trial with spend cap and usage reporting.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm if the token-volume assumptions above look reasonable to your eng team. If off, please send rough daily request/avg token numbers.
- If procurement wants a formal SOW/credit letter, I’ll pull Marissa in to draft it.

Attached: pilot_scope.pdf (short scope), token_projection.xlsx (workload sensitivity).

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick 20-min follow-up tomorrow if helpful.

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0191


From: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple clarifications from our side:
- The 10k requests/day was an overestimate on peak. Our current expected test volume is closer to 3–5k/day (mainly office hours), but we will spike during clinician labeling sessions.
- Average tokens/request is ~1.6k (we do additional prompt engineering to keep context tight).

So revised estimate: ~8M–20M tokens/day during peaks. Still want to proceed with the pilot but procurement will need a clear cap and an expiry on any credit offer.

Procurement asks:
- Can credits be scoped to pilot only and expire after 60 days of activation?
- Can you provide an estimated $/1M token for the model configuration you recommended (including quantization and caching) so we can present numbers to finance?

If you can send a short cost note and the credit terms, I’ll route to procurement. Thanks — Emily

Emily Zhang
Sr. Product Manager, Clinical AI | Horizon Health
emily@horizonhealth.ai | (650) 555-2234


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2026 11:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Emily — thanks for the clarifications, that helps a lot. Short cost note for procurement (feel free to copy into their ticket):

Estimated unit economics (for your workload, conservatively):
- Typical model mix: quantized Llama-3-small variant for embedding/context + Redwood-optimized local reranker.
- Effective price we expect to surface for this pilot: $38 / 1M tokens (this includes conservative allowance for spikes and no long-term commit).

Revised run-rate scenarios (at $38 / 1M):
- 3k req/day * 1.6k tokens = ~4.8M tokens/day -> ~144M tokens/month -> ~$5,472/month.
- 5k req/day * 1.6k tokens = ~8M tokens/day -> ~240M tokens/month -> ~$9,120/month.
- Peak-spike buffer (short experiments): recommend 300k–500k token/day reserve.

Credit ask: Redwood can offer a one-time pilot credit of $12,000 (applies to inference spend only), expiring 60 days after activation. At $38/1M that maps to ~315M tokens of runway and should cover 4–6 weeks of normal testing for the volumes you described.

Marissa, Sean — can you approve a $12k pilot credit for Horizon Health (deal HZ-1172)? Procurement wants the expiry and spend-only-to-inference clause included in the credit letter. I’ve attached the draft SOW/credit note and a token projection sheet.

Thanks — Ben

On Thu, 19 Nov 2026 at 09:10, Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple clarifications from our side: ...

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Ben / Marissa — approved.

- Approval: one-time pilot credit of $12,000 to Horizon Health (deal HZ-1172).
- Terms: credit applies to inference usage only, expires 60 days from date of activation, no rollover, Redwood provides weekly usage report to Horizon.
- Ops: Ben, please set a hard spend cap in the tenant to prevent overages; Marissa, please draft the credit letter and include the expiry/spend-scope language.

Sean Gallagher
VP, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
sean@redwood.inference

title: Activation: stub line repair & spend cap recalibration
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah O'Neil <sarah.oneil@crestline.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Vendor onboarding: provisional PO stubline and spend cap question

Hi Sarah —

Thanks for sending the onboarding form yesterday. I skimmed Crestline's intake packet and noticed the provisional PO you attached is using a placeholder stub line (line item labeled \"STUBLINE-RESERVE\") rather than the final SKU-level items our billing team needs to issue a definitive PO number.

Two asks to unblock:
