title: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ian Thomas <ian.thomas@parkview.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Team —

Parkview hit us this morning after the private cutover at ~03:30 PDT. They’re seeing intermittent application-level session resets and 502s when traffic egresses through their inspection firewall. Customer trace shows a path that sometimes traverses the inspection appliance on return and sometimes bypasses it, which is causing TCP RSTs / stale conntrack entries.

Symptoms from Parkview:
- App logs: short-lived TCP sessions, abrupt RSTs ~30–90s after open.
- User impact: intermittent request failures to the inference endpoint (about 6–8% of requests during peak).
- They provided traceroutes and a pcap (attached in their reply).

Immediate asks:
1) Can infra/SRE check conntrack counters and SNAT port exhaustion on the private gateway (Parkview side and our egress)?
2) Can runtime check for any recent kernel-level MTU/GSO/GRO changes on the on-prem edge that might cause fragmentation?
3) Karthik/Marissa: Quick audit for policy-based routing or asymmetric route-redistribution introduced in the cutover. If asymmetric return path is hitting a stateful inspection device, that’s our likely root.

Proposed quick mitigations (if we confirm asymmetric path):
- Add route pinning to keep return traffic symmetric for the affected /24s (short-term).
- Apply MTU clamp on the inspection appliance or enforce MSS clamp on our egress.
- Coordinate with Parkview to temporarily disable stateful inspection for the affected path for 30 minutes to validate.

I’m looping in Parkview Ops on the thread. Timeboxed: can we meet these checks within 90 minutes? I’ll take on coordination with the customer.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Escalations Lead, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai

---
Attachment: none (requesting captures from customer)


From: Ian Thomas <ian.thomas@parkview.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 10:03:14 -0800
Subject: Re: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Ava —

Thanks for the quick ping. We started collecting traces as soon as the problem surfaced. Uploaded two files to the shared Drive (link below):
- parkview_traceroute_before_after.txt (shows two return paths differing at hop 7)
- parkview_session_capture.pcap (sample of failed session, ~120 KB)

Observations from our side:
- Forward path (client -> Redwood) is stable.
- Return path (Redwood -> client) alternates between the inspection appliance and the MPLS route; when it hits the inspection device we see RSTs and broken sessions.
- We can run a short config change to disable inspection on the affected subnet for testing (no production change without your sign-off).

We’re available for a call now. Details in Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/parkview-cutover-logs

Ian
Ian Thomas
NetOps Lead, Parkview Health
ian.thomas@parkview.com

---
Attachments: parkview_traceroute_before_after.txt, parkview_session_capture.pcap


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Ian Thomas <ian.thomas@parkview.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 12:05:26 -0800
Subject: Re: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Quick triage notes after reviewing the pcap and traceroute:

Findings:
- The PCAP shows the SYN/SYN-ACK handshake completes, but at ~34s we see a RST coming from the inspection appliance IP (consistent with a stale conntrack or TCP reset triggered by deep-packet inspection).
- The traceroute sample demonstrates return-path asymmetry: two return-hop patterns, one via MPLS core (symmetric) and one via inspection appliance (asymmetric).
- conntrack counters on the private gateway (we requested live stats from Parkview) show high TIME_WAIT and a burst of new entries coincident with the cutover window.

Hypothesis:
- The inspection appliance is performing stateful inspection and occasionally receives return packets it never saw the forward flow for (due to asymmetry). It then injects RSTs or drops packets, leaving the client-server sessions inconsistent.
- Secondary contributing factor: SNAT ephemeral port reallocation between paths could be confusing the appliance. MTU/GRO/GSO issues look unlikely from the capture but we should still verify kernel params.

Recommended immediate steps (test window):
1) Parkview: Temporarily bypass stateful inspection for the affected CIDR for 20 minutes (test).
2) On our side: pin routes for the affected /26 to force symmetric egress/ingress via MPLS core for the next 2 hours.
3) Increase conntrack_eviction thresholds on the private gateway as a mitigation to reduce premature state expiry.
4) Collect conntrack dumps pre/post test (Parkview will provide).

I can run the route pin and conntrack tuning if SRE approves; Dev, can you confirm there are no routing filters that will reject a pinned route? Vanessa, please prepare the playbook and runbook for the test and rollback.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Network Reliability, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

---

Subject: Re: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Thanks team —

I ran a quick audit of the BGP export/import policies and there’s a conditional route-map that was introduced during the cutover to advertise the new /26 with a lower local-preference under failover conditions. It looks like the MPLS core and the inspection appliance learned different next-hops for the same prefix depending on which egress was active. That fits the asymmetry pattern.

Action plan (executing now):
- Marissa will apply a temporary static next-hop for the affected prefix at our edge to force symmetric return via MPLS.
- Dev will prepare conntrack parameter bump (net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max + eviction tuning) and hold until we validate.
- Vanessa will coordinate a 30-minute test window with Parkview to disable inspection on the affected CIDR.
- Ava: please coordinate the customer announcement and confirm the test time (propose start at 16:15 PST).

I’ll monitor BGP and conntrack metrics during the window. If the RSTs stop when inspection is bypassed and route pinning is in place, we push for a permanent fix: remove the conditional route-map that causes split-horizon for this prefix or implement symmetric path enforcement in the MPLS fabric.

Karthik

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ian Thomas <ian.thomas@parkview.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Team —

Parkview hit us this morning after the private cutover at ~03:30 PDT. They’re seeing intermittent application-level session resets and 502s when traffic egresses through their inspection firewall. Customer trace shows a path that sometimes traverses the inspection appliance on return and sometimes bypasses it, which is causing TCP RSTs / stale conntrack entries.

Symptoms from Parkview:
- App logs: short-lived TCP sessions, abrupt RSTs ~30–90s after open.
- User impact: intermittent request failures to the inference endpoint (about 6–8% of requests during peak).
- They provided traceroutes and a pcap (attached in their reply).

Immediate asks:
1) Can infra/SRE check conntrack counters and SNAT port exhaustion on the private gateway (Parkview side and our egress)?
2) Can runtime check for any recent kernel-level MTU/GSO/GRO changes on the on-prem edge that might cause fragmentation?
3) Karthik/Marissa: Quick audit for policy-based routing or asymmetric route-redistribution introduced in the cutover. If asymmetric return path is hitting a stateful inspection device, that’s our likely root.

Proposed quick mitigations (if we confirm asymmetric path):
- Add route pinning to keep return traffic symmetric for the affected /24s (short-term).
- Apply MTU clamp on the inspection appliance or enforce MSS clamp on our egress.
- Coordinate with Parkview to temporarily disable stateful inspection for the affected path for 30 minutes to validate.

I’m looping in Parkview Ops on the thread. Timeboxed: can we meet these checks within 90 minutes? I’ll take on coordination with the customer.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Escalations Lead, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai

From: Ian Thomas <ian.thomas@parkview.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 10:03:14 -0800
Subject: Re: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Ava —

Thanks for the quick ping. We started collecting traces as soon as the problem surfaced. Uploaded two files to the shared Drive (link below):
- parkview_traceroute_before_after.txt (shows two return paths differing at hop 7)
- parkview_session_capture.pcap (sample of failed session, ~120 KB)

Observations from our side:
- Forward path (client -> Redwood) is stable.
- Return path (Redwood -> client) alternates between the inspection appliance and the MPLS route; when it hits the inspection device we see RSTs and broken sessions.
- We can run a short config change to disable inspection on the affected subnet for testing (no production change without your sign-off).

We’re available for a call now. Details in Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/parkview-cutover-logs

Ian
Ian Thomas
NetOps Lead, Parkview Health
ian.thomas@parkview.com

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Ian Thomas <ian.thomas@parkview.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 12:05:26 -0800
Subject: Re: Parkview: intermittent session resets after private cutover

Quick triage notes after reviewing the pcap and traceroute:

Findings:
- The PCAP shows the SYN/SYN-ACK handshake completes, but at ~34s we see a RST coming from the inspection appliance IP (consistent with a stale conntrack or TCP reset triggered by deep-packet inspection).
- The traceroute sample demonstrates return-path asymmetry: two return-hop patterns, one via MPLS core (symmetric) and one via inspection appliance (asymmetric).
- conntrack counters on the private gateway (we requested live stats from Parkview) show high TIME_WAIT and a burst of new entries coincident with the cutover window.

Hypothesis:
- The inspection appliance is performing stateful inspection and occasionally receives return packets it never saw the forward flow for (due to asymmetry). It then injects RSTs or drops packets, leaving the client-server sessions inconsistent.
- Secondary contributing factor: SNAT ephemeral port reallocation between paths could be confusing the appliance. MTU/GRO/GSO issues look unlikely from the capture but we should still verify kernel params.

Recommended immediate steps (test window):
1) Parkview: Temporarily bypass stateful inspection for the affected CIDR for 20 minutes (test).
2) On our side: pin routes for the affected /26 to force symmetric egress/ingress via MPLS core for the next 2 hours.
3) Increase conntrack_eviction thresholds on the private gateway as a mitigation to reduce premature state expiry.
4) Collect conntrack dumps pre/post test (Parkview will provide).

I can run the route pin and conntrack tuning if SRE approves; Dev, can you confirm there are no routing filters that will reject a pinned route? Vanessa, please prepare the playbook and runbook for the test and rollback.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Network Reliability, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

title: Site evaluation & offline-ops roadmap for Project Helios
preview:
From: Sean Mitchell <sm@helios.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Project Helios - next steps after today's call

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the call this morning — we appreciated the clarity on deployment options. Per our conversation, Helios is strongly leaning toward an air-gapped private deployment (on-prem appliance) because of the regulatory constraints on patient data and our internal review board. A few follow-ups from our side: 

- We've captured the rough site inventory and I can attach it — the data center has two adjacent half-racks available and private VLANs.
- Upgrade windows: we typically allow planned maintenance 1x/month during a 4-hour window (Sat 02:00–06:00 local). Are there any requirements from Redwood about upgrade cadence or offline patching that would conflict?
- Hardware: curious what the minimum recommended GPU config is for sustained inference at our target qps (150 QPS average, peaks ~500 QPS). We're flexible on chassis form-factor as long as the solution supports offline firmware/BIOS updates.
- Spares & lifecycle: who owns spare parts and firmware images in an air-gapped setup? Do you provide an appliance swap program?

I attached the site-inventory CSV and a one-page network diagram for context. Can we loop in your solutions/ops team to outline a brief roadmap and site-survey checklist so we can finalize a statement of work?

Thanks,
Sean
Sean Mitchell
Head of IT Operations — Helios Therapeutics
sm@helios.ai

Attachments: helios-site-inventory.csv, network-diagram.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sean Mitchell <sm@helios.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 11:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Project Helios - next steps after today's call

Thanks Sean — good signal on requirements, appreciate the inventory and diagram. Recap and proposed next steps below so we have an aligned runbook.

Recap from our call:
- Helios preference: air-gapped (on-prem) deployment for PHI workloads.
- Target load: ~150 QPS sustained, 500 QPS peaks.
- Preferred maintenance window: monthly 4-hour Sat window.

Proposed next steps (action owners in parens):
1) Site-survey kickoff — remote survey first, then on-site visit if required. Please confirm whether Nov 24–25 works for a 90-min remote walkthrough (Sean). (Owner: Ben)
2) Inventory + network artifact collection — we have the CSV; please add power circuit IDs and switch model if possible. (Owner: Helios IT)
3) Offline upgrade / patch process doc — we'll share our air-gapped ops checklist covering image delivery, integrity verification, and required on-site steps. (Owner: Neha)
4) Initial hardware sizing & recommended BOM — Marissa will propose 1) a conservative baseline for your steady qps and 2) a scaled profile for peak capacity. (Owner: Marissa)
5) Draft SOW and timeline — I'll circulate a short SOW (attached v0.1) after we confirm the survey date. (Owner: Ben)

Required artifacts from Helios for the survey:
- Completed site-inventory (thanks — if you can add PDU/circuit IDs that helps)
- Network diagram showing firewall & NAT points, any restricted outbound egress rules
- List of on-call contacts and preferred escalation path during on-site work


Notes on spares and lifecycle: we support two models for air-gapped deployments — a customer-owned spare pool (recommended for rapid replacement) or Redwood-managed spare swaps under a paid support add-on. We'll include both options in the SOW. Neha, Marissa — can you confirm the default spare recommendation and estimated lead times?

I'll tentatively hold 11/24 10:00–11:30 PT for the remote survey; Sean please confirm.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: sow-draft-v1.docx

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Mitchell <sm@helios.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 08:33:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Project Helios - initial HW sizing thoughts (internal note)

Internal note (dropping initial sizing so we can iterate):
- Based on 150 QPS sustained / 500 QPS peaks and assuming 8–12 tokens per request average, initial conservative baseline: 4 x H100-80GB (or equivalent Gen-N) across 1 rack with 2 inference nodes + 2 warm standby nodes.
- For peak handling we recommend: 1) request shaping + batching at the edge, 2) an autoscaling spare pool (hot spare rack) if Helios expects sustained peak windows > 10 minutes.
- Storage: local NVMe for model cache (1–2 TB per node) plus an encrypted on-site object store for logging; no outbound connections required for normal inference ops.
- Upgrades: we can ship signed image bundles on USB or encrypted transfer media for air-gapped installs. On-site operator performs the image verification (SHA256) and the appliance accepts the bundle after M-of-N operator approval.

Neha — please flag anything missing on the ops/upgrade side and confirm typical swap lead times for our recommended spare model. I'll prepare a first-cut BOM and cost delta (baseline vs peak) after we get the full inventory.

-Marissa
Marissa Cole
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

From: Sean Mitchell <sm@helios.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Pauline Zhao <pauline.z@helios.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 10:21:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Project Helios - next steps after today's call

Thanks Ben — confirming the attach (inventory + network diagram) are included. A couple quick clarifications from Pauline (in CC):
- Upgrade cadence: our compliance team will require quarterly major software upgrades at minimum; they are comfortable with monthly security patch windows but want a documented rollback path. How do you handle rollback in an air-gapped appliance?
- Spares: we prefer Redwood to manage a single hot-spare appliance located in our SOC. Is there an estimate for a managed-spare add-on?

Quoted for context:
> Ben Carter wrote:
> > Required artifacts from Helios for the survey:
> > - Completed site-inventory (thanks — if you can add PDU/circuit IDs that helps)
> > - Network diagram showing firewall & NAT points, any restricted outbound egress rules
> > - List of on-call contacts and preferred escalation path during on-site work

We can add the PDU/circuit IDs — attaching updated CSV. On the rollback question: can you share your standard rollback MOP and whether firmware-only rollbacks are supported without reimaging the OS?

Sean

Attachments: helios-site-inventory.csv (updated)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sean Mitchell <sm@helios.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Pauline Zhao <pauline.z@helios.ai>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 15:43:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Project Helios - site evaluation & offline-ops roadmap for Project Helios

Thanks Sean — got the updated inventory. Quick answers and confirmed next steps: 

Rollback & upgrades:
- We support atomic image-based upgrades for the appliance; typical process: upload signed bundle -> verify checksums -> staged install -> validation -> commit. The appliance supports rollback to the previous image if validation fails during the staged install (no need to reimage in most cases). Firmware rollbacks that affect baseboard management are supported but may require a short reboot window; we'll call those out in the maintenance plan.

Managed spare program (high level pricing):
- Redwood-managed hot-spare (onsite in customer SOC): monthly support add-on, includes annual refresh and 3 business-day swap SLA. Ballpark: $Xk monthly (we'll put an exact figure in the SOW).

Confirmed schedule & owners:
- Remote site survey: 2026-11-24 10:00–11:30 PT (confirmed tentative) — Ben, Marissa, Neha attend; Sean + Pauline host.
- Deliverables before survey (Owner - due):
  1) Final inventory CSV with PDU IDs (Helios - done)
  2) Any constrained egress rules / firewall allow-list (Helios - please send by 11/23)
  3) Draft air-gapped ops checklist (Neha - send by 11/23)
  4) Preliminary BOM + sizing note (Marissa - send by 11/23)
  5) Draft SOW v0.2 (Ben - will send by 11/23 with pricing options)

I'll push SOW v0.2 and a short MOP/rollback appendix into the shared drive this afternoon: https://drive.redwood.com/s/helios-site-pack. If liaisons at Helios prefer a call to walk the ops checklist prior to the survey, we can slot 30 minutes on 11/23 morning.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Subject: RE: Project Helios - initial HW sizing thoughts (internal note)

Internal note (dropping initial sizing so we can iterate):
- Based on 150 QPS sustained / 500 QPS peaks and assuming 8–12 tokens per request average, initial conservative baseline: 4 x H100-80GB (or equivalent Gen-N) across 1 rack with 2 inference nodes + 2 warm standby nodes.
- For peak handling we recommend: 1) request shaping + batching at the edge, 2) an autoscaling spare pool (hot spare rack) if Helios expects sustained peak windows > 10 minutes.
- Storage: local NVMe for model cache (1–2 TB per node) plus an encrypted on-site object store for logging; no outbound connections required for normal inference ops.
- Upgrades: we can ship signed image bundles on USB or encrypted transfer media for air-gapped installs. On-site operator performs the image verification (SHA256) and the appliance accepts the bundle after M-of-N operator approval.

Neha — please flag anything missing on the ops/upgrade side and confirm typical swap lead times for our recommended spare model. I'll prepare a first-cut BOM and cost delta (baseline vs peak) after we get the full inventory.

title: EU / UK SCCs: module guidance + evidence pack request
preview:
From: Elisa Martin <elisa.martin@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@acmehealth.com
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: SCCs module recommendation + evidence we need for procurement

Hi Ben —

Quick one from our procurement/legal team as we move towards an executed DPA. Our EU privacy team asked us to confirm which SCCs module Redwood intends to rely on for EU -> UK and other outbound transfers, and to see the supporting evidence pack for the SOC 2 / security controls that the procurement reviewers will want to attach to their review.

Two specific asks:
- Recommend a single SCCs module for us to adopt (our default preference is something that avoids additional subprocessor paperwork where possible).
- A short list of the evidence artifacts you can provide (SOC 2 type II reports, encryption and key management statements, logging/audit sample, and the SCCs execution checklist).

Can you confirm and share a short matrix we can attach to our ticket? Procurement is hoping for something they can include in the vendor scorecard by Friday.

Thanks — appreciate the fast turnaround.

Elisa Martin
Head of Procurement
Acme Health
elisa.martin@acmehealth.com
+1 415-555-0198

Attachments: none


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elisa Martin <elisa.martin@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 11:34:00 -0500
Subject: Re: SCCs module recommendation + evidence we need for procurement

Elisa —

Thanks for the note and the clear asks. Short answer up front: for typical SaaS integrations where Acme is the controller and Redwood is acting as the processor, we recommend adopting the Controller-to-Processor module (Module 2) with the UK Addendum for UK transfers. That keeps the DPA footprint straightforward and avoids unnecessary processor-to-processor obligations that trigger extra subprocesser assessments.

High-level rationale:
- Module 2 maps to our DPA model (Acme controller, Redwood processor) so obligations are aligned with operational controls.
- Using the UK Addendum provides parity for UK transfers without switching to a different execution path.
- If Acme requires cross-processor chaining for specific integrations, we can discuss a processor-to-processor path, but that usually adds complexity for both sides.

I attached a short decision matrix (SCCs-Decision-Matrix-v1.xlsx) and I can also push our evidence pack.

Evidence we can provide immediately (zipped):
- SOC 2 Type II report (most recent period)
- InfoSec control matrix (mapping controls to SCCs clauses)
- Encryption & KMS statement (how keys are managed in hosted/dedicated/private deployments)
- Sample audit logs and retention policy extract
- SCCs execution checklist + DPA redline guidance

Next steps I propose:
1) Monica (legal) will confirm final module recommendation and the minimal DPA Annex text we should include.
2) I’ll share the evidence pack link + a short cover letter you can attach to procurement.
3) If your team wants, we can set a 20-minute sync to walk the procurement reviewer through everything — I can bring Karthik to cover commercial / timelines.

Proposed timeline: evidence pack + matrix delivered by EOD Thu (19 Nov); call Tues if helpful.

Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 212-555-0123

Attachments: SCCs-Decision-Matrix-v1.xlsx, Redwood-Evidence-Pack-2026-11.zip


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 08:47:00 -0500
Subject: RE: SCCs module recommendation + evidence we need for procurement

Ben / Karthik —

Quick legal take to add nuance to Ben’s recommendation: I agree Module 2 (Controller-to-Processor) + UK Addendum is the pragmatic default when Acme is the controller. Pros: clear allocation of responsibilities, minimal subprocess paperwork, matches our standard DPA. Cons to flag for the customer: if they plan to push data onward to downstream processors under their direction, that could push us into a processor-to-processor scenario for specific endpoints — which we should capture in Annex 1 of the DPA rather than change the SCCs module wholesale.

Action items for us / asks for Acme:
- We’ll send a short transfer questionnaire for Acme to confirm transfer flows (who initiates exports, are any downstream processors used, data categories, and special categories).
- I’m attaching our SCCs evidence checklist (SCCs-evidence-checklist.pdf) that maps what we can provide vs what requires a separate attestation.
- Legal will prepare a DPA redline showing Module 2 + UK Addendum inserted and a short cover note explaining the choice.

DocuSign note: once the parties agree the DPA language we can route to DocuSign. If procurement needs a signed cover letter sooner we can produce a cover letter and include the evidence zip while the DPA review completes.

Monica Patel
Sr. Counsel, Privacy & Security | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com

Attachments: SCCs-evidence-checklist.pdf

Thanks — that helps a lot. Acme procurement is comfortable with Module 2 + UK Addendum as the starting point; we’ll complete the short transfer questionnaire and return it to you by Monday. A couple of quick follow-ups:

- Can you include SOC 2 Type II redaction instructions (our auditors require a redaction summary when attaching vendor reports)?

Short answer up front: for typical SaaS integrations where Acme is the controller and Redwood is acting as the processor, we recommend adopting the Controller-to-Processor module (Module 2) with the UK Addendum for UK transfers. That keeps the DPA footprint straightforward and avoids unnecessary processor-to-processor obligations that trigger extra subprocesser assessments.

High-level rationale:
- Module 2 maps to our DPA model (Acme controller, Redwood processor) so obligations are aligned with operational controls.
- Using the UK Addendum provides parity for UK transfers without switching to a different execution path.
- If Acme requires cross-processor chaining for specific integrations, we can discuss a processor-to-processor path, but that usually adds complexity for both sides.

I attached a short decision matrix (SCCs-Decision-Matrix-v1.xlsx) and I can also push our evidence pack.

Evidence we can provide immediately (zipped):
- SOC 2 Type II report (most recent period)
- InfoSec control matrix (mapping controls to SCCs clauses)
- Encryption & KMS statement (how keys are managed in hosted/dedicated/private deployments)
- Sample audit logs and retention policy extract
- SCCs execution checklist + DPA redline guidance

Next steps I propose:
1) Monica (legal) will confirm final module recommendation and the minimal DPA Annex text we should include.
2) I’ll share the evidence pack link + a short cover letter you can attach to procurement.
3) If your team wants, we can set a 20-minute sync to walk the procurement reviewer through everything — I can bring Karthik to cover commercial / timelines.

Proposed timeline: evidence pack + matrix delivered by EOD Thu (19 Nov); call Tues if helpful.

Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 212-555-0123

Attachments: SCCs-Decision-Matrix-v1.xlsx, Redwood-Evidence-Pack-2026-11.zip


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 08:47:00 -0500
Subject: RE: SCCs module recommendation + evidence we need for procurement

Ben / Karthik —

Quick legal take to add nuance to Ben’s recommendation: I agree Module 2 (Controller-to-Processor) + UK Addendum is the pragmatic default when Acme is the controller. Pros: clear allocation of responsibilities, minimal subprocess paperwork, matches our standard DPA. Cons to flag for the customer: if they plan to push data onward to downstream processors under their direction, that could push us into a processor-to-processor scenario for specific endpoints — which we should capture in Annex 1 of the DPA rather than change the SCCs module wholesale.

Action items for us / asks for Acme:
- We’ll send a short transfer questionnaire for Acme to confirm transfer flows (who initiates exports, are any downstream processors used, data categories, and special categories).
- I’m attaching our SCCs evidence checklist (SCCs-evidence-checklist.pdf) that maps what we can provide vs what requires a separate attestation.
- Legal will prepare a DPA redline showing Module 2 + UK Addendum inserted and a short cover note explaining the choice.

DocuSign note: once the parties agree the DPA language we can route to DocuSign. If procurement needs a signed cover letter sooner we can produce a cover letter and include the evidence zip while the DPA review completes.

Monica Patel

monica.patel@redwood.com

title: Proposal: Joint 'Observability Connect' SKU for Grafana + OTLP Relay (pilot)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sophia Rivera <sophia@grafana.com>, otlp-team@opentelemetry.io
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: Joint 'Observability Connect' SKU for Grafana + OTLP Relay (pilot)

Hi Sophia / OTLP team,

Great meeting last week — thanks for taking the time. Following up with a short sketch of a commercial pilot we think could accelerate adoption for both teams. High level: Redwood would ship a packaged OTLP relay (managed by Redwood) plus a lightweight Grafana plugin that simplifies first-run configuration and entitlement. We’d go to market as a co‑branded SKU "Observability Connect" with a pilot to validate integration effort and 3‑month payback via increased Managed Grafana seats + Redwood usage.

Key proposed scope:
- Redwood delivers an OTLP relay appliance (container + Helm chart) and hosted ingestion endpoint.
- Grafana ships a UI plugin that toggles the relay and provisions API keys.
- Single sign-on / entitlement handshake (SAML/OIDC) for provisioning.
- Joint success metrics: pilot onboarding time < 3 days, average ingestion cost per 10k hosts, retention of pilot customers to paid SKU >= 60%.

Commercial thoughts (draft):
- Pilot: 3 months, 3 customers (mutual selection), cost-share on integration dev (Redwood covers runtime; Grafana covers UI dev effort).
- Revenue split on direct SKU sales to existing Grafana Cloud customers: 70/30 (Grafana/Redwood) for first 12 months; revisit after year 1.

Attachments: rdw_grafana_sku_proposal_v1.pdf (high-level deck).

Internal notes (Marcus / Vanessa) — please review scope and add technical caveats.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Partnerships, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0164

--
Redwood Inference | developer-first inference runtime



From: Sophia Rivera <sophia@grafana.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: otlp-team@opentelemetry.io
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Joint 'Observability Connect' SKU for Grafana + OTLP Relay (pilot)

Hi Ava — thanks, this looks promising. A few initial questions from the Grafana product team:

1) What does the entitlement handshake look like? We need to ensure customers can opt in from Grafana Cloud without separate billing atoms.
2) How intrusive is the OTLP relay to a customer's existing OTLP pipeline? Can we do a non-invasive "sidecar" style install for pilot customers?
3) Will Redwood host retention policies or will Grafana manage retention tiers in our UI?

From a go-to-market perspective we like the co-branded angle, but would prefer a 60/40 split in Grafana's favor for the pilot commercial model. If you can provide a short technical spec (1–2 pages) and a proposed pilot candidate list, we can route to revenue ops and legal for an LOI.

Sophia Rivera
Head of Integrations, Grafana Labs
sophia@grafana.com



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Joint 'Observability Connect' SKU for Grafana + OTLP Relay (pilot)

Adding some technical color to share with Grafana: see attached integration-spec-v1.pdf. Short answers:
- Entitlement handshake: we propose a token exchange flow over a webhook + signed JWT issued by Grafana; Redwood validates the JWT and mints a short-lived ingestion key. No additional billing object required for pilot — we can invoice Grafana or do usage pass-through, depending on revenue ops preference.
- Sidecar install: yes. The relay can run as a DaemonSet/sidecar and is transparent to the upstream OTLP source; customers see no schema changes. For hosted trial customers we can also offer a 1-click ingest endpoint bound to their Grafana Cloud org id.
- Retention: configurable by tenant on Redwood side. For the pilot we propose Redwood manage short-term retention (30 days) to simplify the Grafana UI surface; after GA we can add tiered retention controls surfaced in Grafana.

Risks / outstanding items:
- SAML/OIDC provisioning will need an identity mapping table to tie Grafana orgs to Redwood tenants (engineering effort: ~2 weeks).
- e2e compliance (SOC2 evidence) — Grafana legal asked about logs; we can provide current SOC2 snapshots but on-prem pilot customers may require additional controls.

I'll iterate the spec and can push a redline to the deck once we get feedback on the commercial split.

—Marcus
Senior PM, Platform Integrations | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sophia Rivera <sophia@grafana.com>, otlp-team@opentelemetry.io, integrations@datadog.com
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Joint 'Observability Connect' SKU for Grafana + OTLP Relay (pilot)

Sophia — appreciate the quick review and constructive asks. Quick replies inline:

1) Commercial split: Redwood can accept 60/40 Grafana/Redwood for the pilot, provided both teams commit engineers for the 3-month window and Grafana leads entitlement provisioning.
2) Pilot customers: we recommend picking 3 customers across SMB / mid-market / strategic who already use Grafana Cloud and ingest OTLP. I can share a short candidate list this week (will pull two names from our pilot-ready pipeline).
3) Next steps:
   - Marcus to finalize the 2-page technical spec and share by Monday (Nov 23).
   - Legal / RevOps sync: propose a 30-minute call week of Nov 29 to lock pilot commercial terms and billing flow.
   - Success metrics dashboard: Redwood will instrument onboarding steps and share a live Grafana board for pilot tracking.

DocuSign / LOI: https://app.hubspot.com/LOI/rdw-grafana-obsconnect-proposal (placeholder link).

Attachments: integration-spec-v1.pdf.

If that works, Sophia — can your team propose an LOI template and a RevOps contact?

Thanks all — excited to get this moving.

Ava
Partnerships, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0164

--
Attachments referenced: integration-spec-v1.pdf (technical spec), rdw_grafana_sku_proposal_v1.pdf (commercial deck)

title: Co-marketing: DataOps cross-promo roadshow + joint webinar — logistics & MDF

From: Bethany Ortiz <bethany@cloudpartner.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>

Subject: Proposed DataOps Roadshow: co-hosted booth + webinar series

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. CloudCatalyst is planning a regional DataOps roadshow (San Francisco, Austin, Boston) and we think Redwood would be a perfect co-host. High-level we propose: 1) a co-branded booth at each city, 2) a follow-up joint webinar in each region targeting engaged attendees, and 3) shared demand generation (emails + ads).

Budget: we have a standard MDF envelope for partners and can commit $18k per city (marketing execution + lead nurture), plus creative support. We usually split physical booth costs 50/50 unless the partner requests additional services.

Can we schedule a 30-min call next week to align on dates and what Redwood needs for co-branding and lead capture? I attached a short proposal for context (CloudCatalyst_Proposal_Oct18.pdf).

Thanks,
Bethany

CloudCatalyst Inc.

title: Clarify DPA: evidence access windows, subprocessor handoff, and export controls
preview:
From: Nina Patel <nina.patel@medsys.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Oliver Grant <oliver@redwood.io>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: evidence access window + export requirements

Hi Ben,

Following up from today's stand — we have a live investigation where our internal IDS flagged anomalous egress to a third-party service. We need to understand what evidence Redwood can provide if we trigger a formal request under the DPA. A few specific asks: 

- What is Redwood's standard timeline for producing a forensic bundle (metadata, logs, KV cache snapshots) after a formal notice?
- Will subprocessors be able to access raw evidence, or do you gate transfers through Redwood first?
- We have an export-permit requirement for certain medical telemetry in our jurisdiction — do you have a process for cross-border transfer approvals / export-control checks?

We may need the evidence to be staged for law enforcement and for our forensics vendor. Please advise expected SLAs so we can plan escalation/notifications.

Thanks,
Nina Patel
Head of Security Operations
MedSys Health

Attachments: incident-summary-1126.pdf (attached by customer)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Nina Patel <nina.patel@medsys.io>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: evidence access window + export requirements

Hi Nina — thanks for the clear list. I've looped in our security and legal leads to make sure we capture any export/permit constraints. High-level answers below; we can follow with a tailored plan once you confirm the scope (data types, affected endpoints, timeframe).

- Initial acknowledgement: we provide an acknowledgement within 48 hours of a formal request per the DPA.
- Metadata & initial indicators: within 72 hours we can provide a timeline of affected requests, IPs, and high-level indicators.
- Forensic bundle: standard staging target is 10 business days after we receive signed evidence access paperwork and any required export permits. In urgent cases we can accelerate to 5 business days, subject to capacity and legal sign-off.
- Subprocessors: Redwood requires subprocessors to comply with our DPA. We do not hand raw customer PII directly to subprocessors for external transfer unless there is an explicit contractual basis or court order. Subprocessors can assist in-generation and collection under Redwood supervision; final handover is coordinated by Redwood.
- Export controls / import permits: cross-border transfers that include regulated medical identifiers require a legal review. We'll need either (a) your import-permit paperwork or (b) a formal legal exception signed by both parties.

I'm attaching our evidence transfer playbook and a SOC2/ISO extract to give your team more context. Laura / Evelyn / Karthik — please review the proposed timelines and call out anything that would change in MedSys's case.

Next step: Nina, can you confirm the data classes (PHI/non-PHI), affected regions, and whether law enforcement will be involved? If so, which vendor will need staged access?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Accounts, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | (415) 555-0199

Attachments: redwood-evidence-playbook.pdf, soc2-iso-extract.pdf


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 08:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: evidence access window + export requirements

Ben / team — quick technical/legal notes from SecOps: 

- \"Forensic bundle\" semantics: we typically provide a read-only snapshot consisting of
  * structured request logs (timestamp, route, model, region),
  * VM-level diagnostic logs for the implicated hosts,
  * KV-cache metadata (not full token streams unless explicitly required), and
  * hashes of binary artifacts for chain-of-custody.

- Access windows: propose the following for MedSys:
  * Initial metadata (indicators) within 48 hours (agreed)
  * Staged read-only bundle available in 7 business days (we can do 10 if legal needs extra time);
  * Controlled remote access for forensic vendor for 30 days after staging (VPN/temporary creds, audit-logged).

- Subprocessor handoff: we treat subprocessors as internal operators for collection but they do not get external transfer rights. Any outbound transfer to a third-party vendor or law enforcement is executed by Redwood and logged.

- Export control checklist: Laura / Legal will need to confirm whether the data categories trigger a formal export permit. If so, process steps are:
  1) Customer uploads import-permit or signed justification (medsys-import-permit-template.docx attached);
  2) Redwood Legal confirms jurisdictional compliance (target: 3 business days);
  3) SecOps schedules evidence staging (target: 7–10 business days).

Also, recommend we redact direct identifiers (SSNs, MRNs) unless MedSys explicitly requests them and documents legal authority.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Director, Security Operations
Redwood Inference



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Nina Patel <nina.patel@medsys.io>
Cc: Oliver Grant <oliver@medsys.io>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: evidence access window + export requirements

Nina — thanks for the patience while we aligned internally. Summary / agreed path forward below: 

1) Acknowledgement and scope: Redwood will send a formal acknowledgement within 48 hours of your signed evidence request. To prepare, please confirm: data classes affected (PHI vs non-PHI), target date range, and whether law enforcement will have direct remote access.

2) Immediate deliverable (within 48–72 hours): high-level indicators and request metadata. This is not the full bundle but should let you triage.

3) Forensic bundle staging: Target 10 business days after we receive (a) signed evidence access paperwork and (b) any required import-permit or legal exception. If you supply the import-permit PDF and sign the evidence-access addendum, we will aim for 7 business days.

4) Subprocessors & handoff: Subprocessors participate in collection only under Redwood supervision. Redwood executes any outbound transfer; subprocessors will not directly deliver raw PII to your vendor without a legal basis.

5) Export / import permits: Please use the attached medsys-import-permit-template.docx and return with the required fields filled. Laura (cc) will review and get back within ~3 business days. If your counsel prefers, we can accept a signed legal exception in lieu of a permit.

Next steps for MedSys:
- Confirm data classes + affected region
- Upload signed evidence request and import-permit (if applicable) to the Drive link: https://drive.redwood.com/forensics/MS-2345

I'll follow up with a one-page SLA summary and a HubSpot ticket (MS-2345) tracking the milestones. If you need an expedited path, tell us which artifacts you need first and we can prioritize.

Best regards,
Ben

Attachments: redwood-evidence-playbook.pdf, medsys-import-permit-template.docx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Matt O'Neil <matt.oneil@nimbuscare.com>
Date: 2026-11-18T14:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: NimbusCare — Order Form & DPA redlines

Thanks Alex / Matt — I've reviewed the attached order form and DPA redlines. Quick notes (high priority items):

- Roles: we can accept processor-only language for inference requests. Suggest replacing the current generic clause with this sentence: \"Redwood Inference shall act solely as Processor with respect to Customer data processed under the Order Form, consistent with the DPA.\" That keeps the model lifecycle language but clarifies legal position.

- KMS: Our standard for Hosted is Redwood-managed KMS with customer-managed key option (bring-your-own-key) for Dedicated and Private deployments. Proposed language to add to the DPA (short): \"Customer may elect BYOK for Dedicated or Private deployments; for Hosted deployments Redwood will encrypt data at rest using Redwood-managed keys unless Customer requests BYOK and executes the associated order-form option.\"

- SCCs / transfers: the redline references EU SCCs (2021 set). We can support EU SCCs for controller-controller transfers where applicable and will provide the SCC Annex for processor scenarios. We should clarify which entity signatory they want on the SCC (Redwood, Redwood US Sub, Redwood EMEA).

- Transfer-impact addendum: there are two technical asks in the DPA notes (training/retention). I'll put language in the attached DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx to indicate permitted retention windows and a separate checkbox for \"training opt-in\" vs \"training opt-out\" to satisfy NimbusCare's compliance team.

I'll attach a short redline summary (in the tracker) and propose we tidy the order form to include the BYOK option as a selectable line item so procurement can price it.

Attachments: DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx (xlsx, 85KB)

Ben Carter
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Matt O'Neil <matt.oneil@nimbuscare.com>
Date: 2026-11-19T09:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: NimbusCare — Order Form & DPA redlines

Couple of operational clarifications from privacy/engineering: 

- Dataflow: For Hosted customers we do not route raw production traffic to model training pipelines by default. Any retention for training is opt-in and will be surfaced in the order form as an add-on. I'll mark the exact retention windows we can guarantee (30 / 90 / 180 days) in the evidence matrix.

- SCCs: From a practical standpoint we can support EU SCCs + processor annex for EU transfers. If NimbusCare prefers UK adequacy plus a UK addendum, we need to confirm which legal entity (Redwood UK Ltd) will sign. That affects the contact points on the SCC Annex — please confirm preferred signatory.

- Evidence: For SOC2 / audit evidence we can provide: SOC2 Type II report excerpt, a high-level KMS architecture diagram, and a subprocessor inventory snapshot (last 30 days). We will not share sensitive internal keys or secrets; evidence will be delivered through an NDA-governed portal or secure share.

I recommend we present a compact \"Transfer Impact Addendum\" with: 1) selected SCC/UK addendum; 2) retention checkbox; 3) BYOK selection; 4) listed deliverables with ETA (SOC2 excerpt — 5 business days; subprocessor list — immediate).

Laura Bennett
Privacy Counsel, Redwood Inference

Proposed language to add to the DPA (short): \"Customer may elect BYOK for Dedicated or Private deployments; for Hosted deployments Redwood will encrypt data at rest using Redwood-managed keys unless Customer requests BYOK and executes the associated order-form option.\"

- SCCs / transfers: the redline references EU SCCs (2021 set). We can support EU SCCs for controller-controller transfers where applicable and will provide the SCC Annex for processor scenarios. We should clarify which entity signatory they want on the SCC (Redwood, Redwood US Sub, Redwood EMEA).

- Transfer-impact addendum: there are two technical asks in the DPA notes (training/retention). I'll put language in the attached DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx to indicate permitted retention windows and a separate checkbox for \"training opt-in\" vs \"training opt-out\" to satisfy NimbusCare's compliance team.

I'll attach a short redline summary (in the tracker) and propose we tidy the order form to include the BYOK option as a selectable line item so procurement can price it.

Attachments: DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx (xlsx, 85KB)

Ben Carter
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Matt O'Neil <matt.oneil@nimbuscare.com>
Date: 2026-11-19T09:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: NimbusCare — Order Form & DPA redlines

Couple of operational clarifications from privacy/engineering: 

- Dataflow: For Hosted customers we do not route raw production traffic to model training pipelines by default. Any retention for training is opt-in and will be surfaced in the order form as an add-on. I'll mark the exact retention windows we can guarantee (30 / 90 / 180 days) in the evidence matrix.

- SCCs: From a practical standpoint we can support EU SCCs + processor annex for EU transfers. If NimbusCare prefers UK adequacy plus a UK addendum, we need to confirm which legal entity (Redwood UK Ltd) will sign. That affects the contact points on the SCC Annex — please confirm preferred signatory.

- Evidence: For SOC2 / audit evidence we can provide: SOC2 Type II report excerpt, a high-level KMS architecture diagram, and a subprocessor inventory snapshot (last 30 days). We will not share sensitive internal keys or secrets; evidence will be delivered through an NDA-governed portal or secure share.

I recommend we present a compact \"Transfer Impact Addendum\" with: 1) selected SCC/UK addendum; 2) retention checkbox; 3) BYOK selection; 4) listed deliverables with ETA (SOC2 excerpt — 5 business days; subprocessor list — immediate).

Laura Bennett

4) Transfer Impact Addendum: Laura will provide the 3-option retention windows and the list of evidence we can deliver. Evidence will be uploaded to the Drive folder linked here.
5) Proposed meeting: Fri 2026-11-20 10:00 PT — Alex to host a 30 min call to walk the final redline. If that slot doesn't work, propose Mon 2026-11-23 afternoon PT.

Attachments: DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx (updated), Nimbus_OrderForm_v3_redline.docx (copy with comments)

title: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>, procurement@orionai.com
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@finops.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

Team / OrionAI — kicking off a short weekly thread to align sales / finance / infra on the upcoming OrionAI pilot and potential Dedicated commitment. Summary and asks below.

TL;DR:
- OrionAI is projecting a 12–16 week pilot starting mid-Dec; potential reserve ask of 10-20 reserved GPUs (A100 equiv) if pilot converts.
- Need a fleet plan this week: capacity runway, burst cushion, and cost/latency tradeoffs for a 10/15/20 slot commitment.
- Action: please review attached MA plan (Orion_MA_plan_v2.xlsx) and send initial risk / blockers by Tue 11/17.

Key details from Orion (Ana): expected steady state 1400k tokens/day, peak 3x on training/ingest days; multi-region read from us-west + us-east. They asked about quantized model variants and burst credits.

Questions for infra/finops:
- Ben / Marissa: can we run a quick 3-scenario cost / latency table (10/15/20 reserves) and outline the burst escrow policy?
- Laura: if we need to prioritize latency SLO for Orion, what does that mean for slot allocation vs. other reserved deals next quarter?
- Stephanie: any gating on procurement / legal that will push signature beyond Dec 1? If so, what's our fallback?

Attachments: Orion_MA_plan_v2.xlsx (pilot timeline + MOPs)

Thanks — Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@finops.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

On Fri, Nov 13, 2026 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com> wrote:
> TL;DR: OrionAI ... Need a fleet plan this week...

Quick pass from infra side:
- I ran three modeled scenarios on current pool usage (attached quick sheet) — numbers are rough but directional. See commit-scenarios.pdf.
  - 10 reserved slots: handles ~60% steady requests with bursts handled via shared pool; median latency +35ms vs dedicated.
  - 15 reserved slots: ~85% steady; peak days need ~40% burst from shared pool.
  - 20 reserved slots: covers nearly all steady + 80% of peak, small burst buffer needed.
- Cost delta (monthly): 10->15 ~ +28%; 15->20 ~ +18% (per our price curves).
- Operational note: we can enable KV prefix caching for their chat workloads to reduce token compute by ~12% on steady-state — will need model tokenization profiles from Orion to estimate precisely.

Attached: commit-scenarios.pdf (table + simple cost/latency charts). I can refine numbers if we get their token histogram.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Capacity Planning, Redwood Inference


From: Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 08:26:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

Thanks, Aditya and Ben — useful starting point. A few clarifying points from procurement/finops side: 

- Our pilot is constrained budget-wise; we are considering a staged commit: initial 10 reserved slots for 8 weeks, with an option to scale to 20 if we hit the conversion metric (p50 latency <= 120ms and <2% error rate).
- We can provide a token histogram and model flavors by EOD tomorrow.
- Procurement question: what does the billing cadence look like for reserved slots vs burst usage? Also, do you offer a short-term credit/discount if we sign early (we have internal budget windows on 12/01)?

Forwarding our internal pilot profile for context (procurement@orionai.com in cc).

On Tue, Nov 16, 2026 at 11:03 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick pass from infra side...

— Ana Martinez
Director of Product, OrionAI


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>, Raj Patel <raj@finops.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 13:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

Team — digging into the tradeoffs and proposed runways. Key points below, and I attached the weekly-fleet-heatmap.png showing collision windows for Nov–Jan.

1) Priority and preemption: If Orion needs latency SLO (p50 <= 120ms), they'd land in our high-priority bucket. That increases effective reserved consumption (since we won't preempt their slots), so 15 slots behaves more like 18 in practical impact.

2) Recommendation (initial): start with 12 reserved slots + 8 burst credits (banked) for Dec pilot. Rationale: balances their budget constraint and gives room for peak; reduces risk of throttling on ingest days. If conversion metrics are met at week 6, convert to 18 slots.

3) Financial view: Marissa / Raj, rough monthly numbers for 12 slots vs 18 in the attached. Note the burn schedule mismatch if they sign after 12/01 — we should consider a proration policy clause.

4) Runbook asks for Sales: we need a mutual action plan itemizing signature milestones and the metric-based conversion language; also a single point of contact for token histograms and weekly usage reports.

Next steps / owners:
- Marissa: update the financial sheet with proration scenarios and send to Ana by EOD Thu 11/19.
- Ben: refine the latency model using the token histogram once Ana sends it.
- Aditya: coordinate the MAP language with legal and Ana.

Laura Bennett
VP, Product Operations — Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>, Raj Patel <raj@finops.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:48:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

Thanks everyone — good alignment. Quick recap and owners (so we have a single source-of-truth in this thread):

- Agreed near-term plan: propose 12 reserved slots + 8 burst credits for the Dec pilot; conversion cadence at week 6 to trigger up-to-18 slots if metrics met. (Laura / Ben pricing + latency rationale accepted.)
- Owners: Marissa (financial proration scenarios & customer quote) by Thu 11/19; Ben (latency model refinement) after token histogram; Aditya (MAP language with legal + send draft to Ana).
- Ana: please send token histogram and model variant list by EOD Wed 11/18 to unblock Ben.

I'll book a 30-min readout for Thu 11/19 10:00–10:30 PT to finalize the MAP and pricing. Calendar invite to follow.

Attachments: weekly-fleet-heatmap.png (collision windows), commit-scenarios.pdf

— Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference

Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Integrated API namespace addition and term adjustment

Hi Alyssa —

Quick follow from our renewal conversation: Dispatch Health is planning to bring two additional internal projects onto the platform (CareFlows & IntakeAPI). To make this low friction, we propose: 

- Create two new API namespaces (one per project) and issue project-scoped API keys.
- Reserve a small dedicated capacity pool to cover baseline throughput for both (target: 50–80 rps combined during peak).
- Apply a short-term onboarding credit (applied to new-project usage for first 90 days).
- Amend the renewal to reflect the incremental committed capacity and a one-line SOW addendum.

Attached: Draft-SOW_DispatchHealth_v2.pdf and an initial API-Key-Matrix.csv (blank template).

If that sounds right, Karthik and I can outline a rollout week-by-week and coordinate the DocuSign route. Ask: can you confirm procurement/contact for the SOW and whether a formal PO will be required up front?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evan Morales <evan.morales@dispatchhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 11:00:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integrated API namespace addition and term adjustment

Ben — thanks for the clear summary. A few quick Qs before we run this with procurement: 

- Can you confirm how the 90-day onboarding credit shows up on invoices? Line item or one-off credit?
- What’s the expected lead time for provisioning the dedicated capacity pool? We have a go-live window in early December and need keys provisioned before Nov 30.
- Procurement: I’ll loop in our vendor ops (I’m copying Evan) — they’ll want the SOW and a commercial contact. We typically need a PO for new committed spend.

Also: any SOC2 evidence and data-retention terms we should share with our security team?

Best,
Alyssa Kim
Senior PM, Integrations — Dispatch Health
alyssa.kim@dispatchhealth.com

To: Alyssa Kim <alyssa.kim@dispatchhealth.com>

Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 13:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integrated API namespace addition and term adjustment

Thanks, Alyssa — good questions. Short answers below and a proposed rollout calendar. Karthik, Monica — tagging you for capacity and legal/ops notes.

Invoices/credits:
- The onboarding credit is applied as an invoice line item credit labeled “New Project Onboarding Credit (90d)”. It will show on your next invoice following the credit application period and also appear in the Console credits ledger.

Provisioning lead time:
- We can reserve the dedicated capacity pool within 3 business days of SOW signature + required PO. Keys can be generated that same day once provisioning is scheduled. Target: key delivery by Nov 25 if SOW signed by Nov 20.

SOC2/data & compliance:
- SOC2 report and evidence bundle are available via our compliance portal (see link below). Laura can confirm retention windows and encryption-at-rest details.

Proposed rollout (high level):
Week 0 (Nov 20): Finalize SOW and sign addendum (DocuSign).
Week 1 (Nov 22–Nov 26): Provision capacity; generate project-scoped keys; small technical kickoff with Dispatch Eng.
Week 2 (Nov 29–Dec 3): Sandbox validation and staged traffic routing; observe latency & error budgets.
Week 3 (Dec 6): Production flip for CareFlows; IntakeAPI scheduled for Dec 13.

Attached an updated SOW (Draft-SOW_DispatchHealth_v2.pdf) with the committed capacity line and an API-Key-Matrix.csv template pre-filled with the two new namespaces.

Next steps for procurement: if Dispatch can send a PO referencing deal DH-2026-Q4-EXP-009 and sign the addendum, we’ll reserve the pool immediately.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Alyssa Kim <alyssa.kim@dispatchhealth.com>

Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 10:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integrated API namespace addition and term adjustment

Ben/Team — quick alignment from Vendor Ops: 

- Timeline looks good; we can aim for SOW signature by Nov 20 but procurement needs the final SOW and a point of contact for billing.
- A couple of asks from Security/Platform: Dispatch requires a documented latency SLO for dedicated capacity (p95 < 250ms for token generate on standard requests) and a copy of your most recent SOC2 Type II.
- If those are acceptable we’ll route a PO this week.

Evan

title: Hosting evaluation for NovaHealth pilot - Dedicated vs Private sizing
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Amira Khan <amira.khan@novahealth.com>, Paul Lopez <paul.lopez@novahealth.com>
Cc: procurement@novahealth.com
Date: 2026-11-20T10:15:00-08:00
Subject: Dedicated vs Private hosting evaluation for NovaHealth pilot

Hi Amira / Paul —

Thanks for taking the call yesterday. Per NovaHealth's pilot scope, you mentioned two hard constraints: (1) PHI-processing on day-one (so an isolated network requirement), and (2) a Q4 go-live window for the pilot with an anticipated 4–6 week evaluation.

To move this forward from our side we need a short set of signals so we can do a comparative sizing and procurement path (Dedicated reserved GPUs vs Private/VPC or fully on-prem):
- Expected concurrent users and peak RPS (approx)
- Typical prompt length (tokens) and variance (50–1k tokens?)
- Latency SLO targets (p95 latency goal)
- Requirement for offline on-prem processing vs VPC-only
- Any SOC2 / audit evidence timing constraints for attestation

I attached a short requirements summary and a Mutual Action Plan template for your procurement team to fill (attachments: nova_requirements_summary.pdf, MAP-template-v2.docx). Once we have the numbers we can run two outputs: (A) Dedicated reserved capacity slate with cost + commit path, and (B) Private deployment sizing with procurement timeline and installation assumptions.

Can you share the above by EOD Wednesday? If easier, a single row spreadsheet with the fields above works.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
attachments: nova_requirements_summary.pdf, MAP-template-v2.docx


From: Amira Khan <amira.khan@novahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Paul Lopez <paul.lopez@novahealth.com>, procurement@novahealth.com
Date: 2026-11-20T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated vs Private hosting evaluation for NovaHealth pilot

Hi Ben —

Thanks — attaching our quick answers and the PII handling checklist (attached: nova_piihandling_matrix.xlsx). High level answers below:
- Peak concurrent users: 120–200 during pilot ramp; expected peak RPS 180–250 (spikey around business hours).
- Prompt lengths: median ~180 tokens, 95th ~800 tokens (we do some longer document summarization requests occasionally).
- Latency SLO: p95 <= 450ms for single-turn inference (we can tolerate background batches up to 1200ms for async jobs).
- Hosting constraints: must be within a private VPC with customer-controlled routing and KMS for keys; on-prem deployment acceptable if we can do a 4–6 week install.
- SOC2 evidence: we need SOC2 type II or equivalent evidence and an engineer point-of-contact for an audit in Jan.

Timeline: Pilot kickoff targeted the week of 2026-12-06. Procurement lead time: we need pricing and a commitment path by 2026-11-30 to align approvals.

I've copied procurement for formal intake. Attached is the filled PII matrix and a short notes doc. Happy to hop on a 30-min sizing sync if that helps.

—Amira
Amira Khan
Director, Product Security | NovaHealth
attachments: nova_piihandling_matrix.xlsx

> 
> Thanks for taking the call yesterday. Per NovaHealth's pilot scope...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Cc: Amira Khan <amira.khan@novahealth.com>

Subject: Fwd: Dedicated vs Private hosting evaluation for NovaHealth pilot — sizing request

Team — forwarding Amira's responses. TL;DR: 180–250 RPS peak, p95<=450ms target, VPC with KMS required, SOC2 evidence needed, and a four-week window to price/commit. Procurement needs numbers by 11/30.

Can you advise:
- Rough GPU slate sizing (min viable for pilot vs gold configuration) and whether Dedicated reserved capacity or Private on-prem gives us a cleaner procurement path given Nova's SOC2 timing. Include rough monthly cost delta and estimated lead times for each option.
- Any blockers if we provision Dedicated with isolated tenant and customer-managed KMS (our Dedicated + VPC peering config)? Is there additional engineering work to satisfy their SOC2 audit request in Jan?

I attached the filled worksheet from Nova and our MAP template (attachments: nova_piihandling_matrix.xlsx, MAP-template-v2.docx).

Thanks — need a quick internal recommendation to share with Amira by EOD Monday (2026-11-23).

Ben

> Date: 2026-11-20 11:02
> Subject: Re: Dedicated vs Private hosting evaluation for NovaHealth pilot
> To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
> Cc: Paul Lopez <paul.lopez@novahealth.com>, procurement@novahealth.com
>
> Hi Ben —
> 
> Thanks — attaching our quick answers and the PII handling checklist...


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Amira Khan <amira.khan@novahealth.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T15:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Dedicated vs Private hosting evaluation for NovaHealth pilot — sizing request

Ben / Vivek —

Quick sizing + recommendation (high level):

Min viable (pilot):
- Serving: 2x H100-80GB (or 4x A100-80GB with partitioning) in a continuously-batched Dedicated pool. Estimated sustained capacity handles ~200 RPS with median tokens and aggressive batching.
- Cache & Batching config: prefix KV caching for templates + dynamic batching up to 128 requests; this is critical to meet p95<=450ms.
- Cost delta: Dedicated reserved (3-month commit) ≈ $48k/month vs Private (VPC hosted on isolated subnets with customer-managed KMS & SOC2 prep) ≈ $72k/month (higher due to networking, egress safeguards, and additional on-call engineering/ops).

Gold (scale + redundancy):
- Serving: 4x H100 across zonal pools with standby capacity for failover; autoscaling margin.
- Adds ~30% on cost, faster failover and lower p99 SLA exposure.

Recommendation: Start pilot on Dedicated reserved capacity with isolated tenant + customer-managed KMS and pre-agreed SOC2 audit support. Rationale: fastest procurement path (we can offer a 3-month reserve with immediate availability), lower up-front engineering lift, and we can provide SOC2 artifacts and an SOC2 readiness checklist to support the Jan audit. If Nova still requires on-prem after pilot, convert to Private with a planned on-prem installation in Jan/Feb.

Engineer notes / blockers:
- We need a security runbook exchange and proof-of-key-separation (KMS integration details) — estimate 1–2 weeks of SRE/infosec time to validate controls.
- If Nova insists on fully air-gapped on-prem immediately, lead time is 6–8 weeks for on-prem packaging + validation (likely misses their 12/06 pilot kickoff).

Next steps (for Ben):
1) I'll pull a Dedicated slate (nova_dedicated_slate.xlsx) with pricing tiers: 3-month commit vs 12-month commit and an alternate gold config.
2) Vivek, can you confirm whether legal needs an amendment for KMS handling on Dedicated?
3) Ben to share the recommended slate + MAP back to Amira by 11/23.

Lauren Bishop
Sr. Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
attachments: nova_dedicated_slate.xlsx

title: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack
preview:
From: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Soto <elena@lumena.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and the concrete next steps we discussed so your team can share with stakeholders. I pasted the Redwood-verified performance snapshots and links below.

TL;DR (recommendation)
- Pilot: 2-week sandbox to validate two model families: rw-instruct-13b-q4f16 (cost-balanced) and rw-70b-8k-q4 (quality option)
- Target SLOs you mentioned: p99 latency <= 250ms for single-turn API, aggregate throughput ~150–200 TPS for peak windows
- We can support a Dedicated VPC pilot; Ben will scope infra and sandbox access.

Call highlights / constraints
- Primary UX: short chat + contextual retrieval (mostly <1k prompt tokens, often repeated prefixes)
- Data sensitivity: customer PII present in prompts; you prefer VPC/private control plane
- Budget: sizing exercise needed — you flagged wanting committed throughput pricing for budgeting

Recommended models (initial slate)
- rw-instruct-13b-q4f16 (Redwood-verified) — balanced quality/cost, good for retrieval-augmented generation. Typical perf (Redwood-hosted, H100-optimized): 220 TPS aggregate on a 4-GPU pool; latencies: p50 18 ms, p95 88 ms, p99 162 ms.
- rw-70b-8k-q4 (Redwood-verified, quantized) — higher generation quality; perf: 48 TPS; latencies: p50 38 ms, p95 160 ms, p99 310 ms.
- redwood/embed-2-small — vector model for retrieval (throughput >1k EPS on a small cluster).

Notes on profiles above
- Numbers are measured with Redwood's continuous-batching runtime and KV-prefix caching enabled; they reflect typical conversational distributions (mix of 64–512 output tokens).
- For your prefix-heavy flows, caching reduces observed p99 by ~20–35% depending on reuse patterns. We'll provide traces to show this.

Attachments & links
- Attached: pilot-proposal-lumena.pdf (scope + mutual action plan), lumena-prompt-pack-v1.json (sample prompt sets we recommend for regression tests), redwood-verified-profiles.csv (raw perf traces)
- Perf/console links: https://app.redwood.ai/models/rw-instruct-13b/perf-profile and https://app.redwood.ai/models/rw-70b/perf-profile

Proposed next steps
1) Agree pilot scope (2 weeks): baseline prompt set, 10k user-simulated queries, 1 endpoint per model variant.
2) Redwood to stand up sandbox (Dedicated VPC option) — Ben to share sandbox access + cost estimate.
3) Procurement: we can provide a short PO + SOW for the pilot; Neha will follow up on contract questions and SOC2 evidence.
4) Kickoff: proposed window Nov 30–Dec 11 (pilot start).

Would you prefer we schedule a 30-minute pilot kickoff next week to lock scope? If so, I can send a calendar invite with Ben and Neha.

Thanks,
Markus
Markus Klein
Head of GTM Engineering
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: pilot-proposal-lumena.pdf (PDF, 2 pages), lumena-prompt-pack-v1.json (JSON, 1.2 KB), redwood-verified-profiles.csv (CSV, 85 KB)

To: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Thanks Markus — this is very helpful. A few clarifying questions from our side before we share with procurement: 

1) Are the TPS/latency numbers you listed for hosted Redwood (multi-tenant) or for a Dedicated pool? We need p99 numbers for Dedicated VPC since that's our compliance requirement.
2) Can you share the perf traces for the prefix-caching example you mentioned? I want to show the delta to our infra folks.
3) For the 70B variant: is the 310 ms p99 measured with streaming enabled? We care about perceived latency for end users.
4) Procurement asked for SOC2 evidence and a short template for our PO — can you point us to the right place?

If you can respond with the traces and a short estimate for committed monthly TPS pricing for the Dedicated option, we'll loop procurement and schedule the kickoff.

Best,
Elena Soto
Director of Product
Lumena AI

To: Elena Soto <elena@lumena.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Elena — quick answers and attached traces.

1) Dedicated vs hosted: the numbers I quoted in the first mail are from Redwood-hosted baselines on H100 hardware. For Dedicated VPC we run the same runtime but with isolated pools — expect similar p50/p95 latency and slightly improved p99 due to reduced noisy neighbors. I'll attach a short Dedicated snapshot for the 13B variant.

2) Perf traces: attached redwood-verified-profiles.csv (sampled traces) and a small latency trace for the prefix reuse case: https://s3.redwood.ai/public/lumena/prefix-trace-20261117.csv
   - In our prefix reuse test (70% prefix reuse), KV caching reduced p99 by ~28% and median by ~12% compared to no-cache.

3) 70B measurements: the 310 ms p99 I quoted is for non-streaming (full-response latency). With streaming enabled and aggressive chunk sizes you'll see perceived first-byte latencies drop to ~120–160 ms for many prompts; end-to-end completion will still depend on token count.

4) SOC2 / procurement: SOC2 Type II report and standard pilot SOW template are here: https://drive.redwood.ai/security/soc2-type2-2026.pdf and https://drive.redwood.ai/templates/pilot-sow-lumena.docx

Action items I'm taking:
- Ben: please provision a Dedicated sandbox (VPC) and share credentials + a short cost estimate for a 4-GPU pool sized to ~150 TPS baseline.
- Neha: I'll let you follow up on contract/PO language and any compliance questions.
- Elena: we can lock pilot dates Nov 30–Dec 11 as proposed; Ben will propose exact slots once sandbox is up.

Ben (cc) — can you confirm sandbox ETA and a rough monthly committed-TPS price for a modest 150 TPS committed tier?

Thanks,
Markus

text:
From: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
