Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and the concrete next steps we discussed so your team can share with stakeholders. I pasted the Redwood-verified performance snapshots and links below.

TL;DR (recommendation)
- Pilot: 2-week sandbox to validate two model families: rw-instruct-13b-q4f16 (cost-balanced) and rw-70b-8k-q4 (quality option)
- Target SLOs you mentioned: p99 latency <= 250ms for single-turn API, aggregate throughput ~150–200 TPS for peak windows
- We can support a Dedicated VPC pilot; Ben will scope infra and sandbox access.

Call highlights / constraints
- Primary UX: short chat + contextual retrieval (mostly <1k prompt tokens, often repeated prefixes)
- Data sensitivity: customer PII present in prompts; you prefer VPC/private control plane
- Budget: sizing exercise needed — you flagged wanting committed throughput pricing for budgeting

Recommended models (initial slate)
- rw-instruct-13b-q4f16 (Redwood-verified) — balanced quality/cost, good for retrieval-augmented generation. Typical perf (Redwood-hosted, H100-optimized): 220 TPS aggregate on a 4-GPU pool; latencies: p50 18 ms, p95 88 ms, p99 162 ms.
- rw-70b-8k-q4 (Redwood-verified, quantized) — higher generation quality; perf: 48 TPS; latencies: p50 38 ms, p95 160 ms, p99 310 ms.
- redwood/embed-2-small — vector model for retrieval (throughput >1k EPS on a small cluster).

Notes on profiles above
- Numbers are measured with Redwood's continuous-batching runtime and KV-prefix caching enabled; they reflect typical conversational distributions (mix of 64–512 output tokens).
- For your prefix-heavy flows, caching reduces observed p99 by ~20–35% depending on reuse patterns. We'll provide traces to show this.

Attachments & links
- Attached: pilot-proposal-lumena.pdf (scope + mutual action plan), lumena-prompt-pack-v1.json (sample prompt sets we recommend for regression tests), redwood-verified-profiles.csv (raw perf traces)
- Perf/console links: https://app.redwood.ai/models/rw-instruct-13b/perf-profile and https://app.redwood.ai/models/rw-70b/perf-profile

Proposed next steps
1) Agree pilot scope (2 weeks): baseline prompt set, 10k user-simulated queries, 1 endpoint per model variant.
2) Redwood to stand up sandbox (Dedicated VPC option) — Ben to share sandbox access + cost estimate.
3) Procurement: we can provide a short PO + SOW for the pilot; Neha will follow up on contract questions and SOC2 evidence.
4) Kickoff: proposed window Nov 30–Dec 11 (pilot start).

Would you prefer we schedule a 30-minute pilot kickoff next week to lock scope? If so, I can send a calendar invite with Ben and Neha.

Thanks,
Markus
Markus Klein
Head of GTM Engineering
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: pilot-proposal-lumena.pdf (PDF, 2 pages), lumena-prompt-pack-v1.json (JSON, 1.2 KB), redwood-verified-profiles.csv (CSV, 85 KB)

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-11-15T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Pen test report validity + scope questions

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security team has a couple of follow-ups before we sign the SOW: primarily around your latest penetration test — who performed it, how you define the scope (internet-facing vs. authenticated apps), and how recent the report is relative to our procurement window. We typically require a red-team/pen-test dated within 12 months of contract signature and evidence that critical/high findings have remediation verification.

Can you share the report or an evidence package (redacted if needed) and a short note on cadence and whether you offer an attestation for the retest?

Best,
Sam
Sam Jenkins
Senior InfoSec Manager
Zenith Health

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.example>
To: Sam Jenkins <sam.jenkins@zenithhealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.example>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-11-15T11:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Pen test report validity + scope questions

Sam —

Thanks for the clear ask. Short answers up front:
- Vendor: Aegis RedTeam LLC (independent third-party, CREST-aligned testers). We’ve included their one-page credentials in the attachment.
- Scope: the last full engagement included external internet-facing perimeter + authenticated application flow for our inference API (OAuth client flows + service-to-service endpoints). We excluded subcontractor-managed datacenter internals (we can add on if you need).
- Date/cadence: most customers accept a pen test within 12 months; our policy is annual baseline testing with targeted retests after major infra or product changes (e.g., new model-serving path or KV cache rewrite).
- Evidence: we can provide a redacted report plus an evidence index that maps findings to remediation tickets and verification steps.

I’m attaching: redwood-pt-engagement-brief.pdf (summary + scope), aegis-screds-and-scoped-methodology.pdf, and a sample pt-evidence-index.csv. We can also schedule a short walkthrough with Aegis to talk methodology and Q&A.

Action items I propose:
1) I’ll share the redacted report and evidence index (attached).
2) If Zenith requires a retest or expanded scope, we’ll provide a SOW addendum and proposed dates.
3) Lauren and Mina — can you confirm typical internal acceptance windows for Zenith-style vendors (12 months vs. vendor-dependent)?

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.example
+1 415 555 0198

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.example>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.example>, Sam Jenkins <sam.jenkins@zenithhealth.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-11-16T08:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Pen test report validity + scope questions

Hi all — quick note from security ops perspective.

We generally align with the 12-month window for SOC2/commercial reviews, but there are two important nuances:
- If there were critical/high findings in the test, we require documented retest evidence for the fixed items (not just ticket status). An attestation from the vendor that fixes were validated is acceptable when paired with our internal verification.
- For authenticated flow coverage, we expect scope to include at least one full end-to-end scenario (login -> generate key -> decode response). If Zenith has specific flows you want in scope, send a short list and we can scope the addendum.

Lauren Bishop
Director, Security Operations | Redwood Inference

Attachment: none

Sam —

Thanks for the clear ask. Short answers up front:
- Vendor: Aegis RedTeam LLC (independent third-party, CREST-aligned testers). We’ve included their one-page credentials in the attachment.
- Scope: the last full engagement included external internet-facing perimeter + authenticated application flow for our inference API (OAuth client flows + service-to-service endpoints). We excluded subcontractor-managed datacenter internals (we can add on if you need).
- Date/cadence: most customers accept a pen test within 12 months; our policy is annual baseline testing with targeted retests after major infra or product changes (e.g., new model-serving path or KV cache rewrite).
- Evidence: we can provide a redacted report plus an evidence index that maps findings to remediation tickets and verification steps.

I’m attaching: redwood-pt-engagement-brief.pdf (summary + scope), aegis-screds-and-scoped-methodology.pdf, and a sample pt-evidence-index.csv. We can also schedule a short walkthrough with Aegis to talk methodology and Q&A.

We generally align with the 12-month window for SOC2/commercial reviews, but there are two important nuances:
- If there were critical/high findings in the test, we require documented retest evidence for the fixed items (not just ticket status). An attestation from the vendor that fixes were validated is acceptable when paired with our internal verification.
- For authenticated flow coverage, we expect scope to include at least one full end-to-end scenario (login -> generate key -> decode response). If Zenith has specific flows you want in scope, send a short list and we can scope the addendum.

I’m very sorry to report that during the LTL transfer this morning we discovered moisture damage to one of the outer pallets containing the embroidered denim jackets for BrightStream’s sponsor kits. Rough count: approx 160 units affected of the 1,000 total (mostly collars and sleeve embroidery mis-stitching after the package got wet). We pulled random samples and attached photos.

- Full reprint of the damaged qty (recommended for consistent branding)

title: Align on evaluation kickoff + technical deep dive availability and success metrics
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@medicahealth.com>, Tom Evans <tom.evans@medicahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 16:22:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery recap + proposed slots for eval kickoff and tech deep dive

Hi Claire / Tom —

Thanks again for the call today — great to hear how MedicaHealth is thinking about using large language models to accelerate coding of discharge summaries and to triage inbound clinical messages. Quick recap from our side and proposed next steps so we can lock the evaluation kickoff and the technical deep dive: 

Recap (high level)
- Goal: reduce manual discharge coding time by 40% and maintain >95% accuracy on coded fields for first-pass outputs.
- Data: de-identified historical notes (~120k docs) for prompt tuning; integrations: S3 export -> Redwood Private during evaluation.
- Success signals: prompt-level accuracy on held-out set, latency within 200ms/token for 99th pct, and end-to-end throughput to support 5 concurrent users.

Proposed next meetings
1) Eval kickoff (90m) — review mutual action plan, trial configuration, prompt set and evaluation criteria.
2) Technical deep dive (60m) — engineering run-through: access, SSO, ingestion pipeline, and security questions (data retention, audit logs).

Proposed slots (PST) — please pick one for the kickoff and we’ll schedule the deep dive the following day: 
- Tue Nov 23, 10:00–11:30 AM PST
- Thu Nov 25, 1:00–2:30 PM PST

Prep we’ll bring to the kickoff
- Suggested prompt pack + initial evaluation harness (we'll share before the call)
- Trial plan draft with timeline and owners (HubSpot link below)
- Security playbook for Private deployment (SOC2 items highlighted) — can review during tech deep dive.

Attachments: discovery-recap.pdf (notes), proposed-kickoff-slots.ics
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/contact/987654321
Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1abcDEF_kickoff-plan

If the slots above don’t work, send 2 alternatives and we'll accommodate. Once you confirm the kickoff slot I’ll send calendar invites and a short pre-reading pack (prompt examples + evaluation checklist).

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0117



From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@medicahealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Evans <tom.evans@medicahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:03:00 -0800

title: Escalation: Nonstandard indemnity & warranty language — exec waiver required
preview:
From: Claire Liu <claire.liu@clearpath.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@clearpath.ai>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@clearpath.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 14:23:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Proposed Redwood agreement — indemnity and warranty requests

Ben —

Thanks for the draft. Legal here at ClearPath reviewed and we have two substantive asks that block signature on our side:

1) Indemnity: we need the vendor to indemnify ClearPath for third-party IP claims related to the Service (not just direct infringement), and the clause should be unlimited or at least uncapped for IP claims.

2) Warranty & Remedies: we need a full warranty covering accuracy of model outputs for a 12 month period and a right to terminate and receive a refund if material defects persist.

We appreciate that these may be nonstandard; please escalate if Redwood needs exec approval. Were on a tight timeline for our Q1 feature launch and would like to close before Nov 30.

Claire
Claire Liu
Head of Procurement, ClearPath Analytics
Attach: procurement_review_summary.pdf (summary file)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.inference>
Cc: Claire Liu <claire.liu@clearpath.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -05:00
Subject: Fwd: Proposed Redwood agreement — indemnity and warranty requests

Marissa / Dev —

Forwarding ClearPath's legal asks. Short take: their IP indemnity ask is broader than we accept (unlimited IP indemnity + no cap). Warranty ask for 12 months of accuracy with termination/refund is also outside our standard.

Context: this is a mid-market AI analytics customer, potential annual ARR $680k (first year). They've been ready to sign, procurement surfaced the legal items today. They cite regulatory downstream risk and insist on uncapped IP liability and a 12m warranty.

Proposed escalation options (please pick one):
- A: Exec waiver to allow a carve-out: Unlimited indemnity only for ClearPath's direct customers' IP claims involving ClearPath-owned IP; but cap liability at $2M for other claims.
- B: Mutual IP indemnity, cap at $2M, but include an explicit data-scope carveout (we're not responsible for user-supplied prompt content).
- C: Stand firm on standard terms; offer a limited commercial concession (5% first-year credit) and replacement support SLAs.

Im asking for an executive/legal sign-off if we pursue A or B. Attaching my summary redline.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 415-555-0192
Attach: ben_summary_redline.docx

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.inference>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Proposed Redwood agreement — indemnity and warranty requests

Ben / Dev —

Thanks for the quick triage. Ive reviewed ClearPaths asks and the attached redline. This is high risk for three reasons:
1) Unlimited IP indemnity opens us to outsized exposure for model outputs where large models incorporate third-party content.
2) A 12-month accuracy warranty with refund/termination is operationally unbounded and hard to measure (what is "accuracy" for a generative model?).
3) This customer will expect similar concessions in future renewals if we set precedent.

However: the account size and strategic fit (they are a reference for analytics vertical) mean we should consider a narrowly tailored exception rather than walking away. My recommended path:
- Ask GC to approve a conditional exec waiver that permits a modified indemnity limited to: (i) IP claims that demonstrably arise from Redwoods code (not model hallucinations tied to customer prompts), (ii) a specific $2M aggregate cap, and (iii) carveouts for user-supplied data and third-party model components.
- Decline their 12m accuracy warranty as written. Offer instead a 90-day service warranty for platform defects, plus an SLA-based remediation schedule and a commercial credit linked to verified defect severity.

Request: Evelyn — can you weigh in with legal/exec sign-off for a $2M cap with the carveouts above? If you approve, Legal will produce precise redline language and Ill circulate to ClearPath. Time sensitivity: they want to sign before Nov 30.

Marissa Cole
VP, Sales Operations, Redwood Inference
Attach: risk_memo_482.pdf

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Proposed Redwood agreement — indemnity and warranty requests

Marissa / Ben —

Thanks for the summary and the options. After consulting with GC and running downside scenarios with Finance, well approve a limited, one-off executive waiver with these constraints:

1) Indemnity: Redwood will accept indemnity for third-party IP claims that are demonstrably caused by Redwoods proprietary service code or platform defects. Indemnity will be mutual where appropriate.
2) Cap: Aggregate liability limited to $2,000,000 for this contract year (not per-claim).
3) Warranty: No 12-month accuracy warranty. We will provide a 90-day warranty for platform defects plus defined SLAs and a remediation plan.
4) Carveouts: Explicit carveouts for (a) user-provided prompts and outputs, (b) third-party models and components beyond Redwoods control, and (c) consequential damages except where mandated by law.
5) Approval is contingent on Legal producing precise redline text and Risk signoff. It is also contingent on a 1-year term minimum and inclusion of a clawback provision if customer re-negotiates wider terms later.

Next steps (Ben): please share the final redlined agreement when Legal completes it. Im authorizing Legal to insert the approved language and sign off on this one-off exception.

Evelyn Hart
Chief Revenue Officer, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Attach: redlined_contract_v3.pdf; risk_score_snapshot.csv

Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 08:55:00 -08:00
Subject: Draft redline for ClearPath (DL-2026-0482)
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>

(See attached draft language. Legal has inserted mutual indemnity, $2M cap, and the carveouts referenced above.)

title: RBAC & support access — post-demo checklist and clarifications
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ramos <elena@clearline.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T14:12:00-08:00
Subject: RBAC & support access — post-demo checklist and clarifications

Hi Elena,

Thanks again for the demo call today — appreciate the frank conversation about how Clearline handles customer PII and incident response.

Quick recap and clarifying questions we promised to follow up on (answers will help scope implementation + any contract attachments):

1) PII scope: can you confirm the categories of data you consider PII for this workload (names, emails, SSNs, account numbers, transaction metadata, voice transcripts)?
2) Retention: target retention windows for raw inputs, generated outputs, and audit logs (what must be retained for compliance vs what can be auto-deleted)?
3) Residency: which geographic region(s) must data and logs reside in (US-only, EU-only, hybrid)?
4) Support access model: current least-privilege controls for support engineers (role tiers, just-in-time elevation, approval workflow)?
5) Break-glass: you mentioned a two-person approval for emergency access — can you confirm approvers are internal Clearline SRE + security manager, and whether approval can be via DocuSign or an internal ticketing workflow?
6) Logging & evidence: required log retention duration and whether logs must be exported to your SIEM (Splunk/Chronicle) or if Redwood-hosted logs are acceptable.

Attachments: mutual-action-plan.xlsx (proposed timeline + gating), redwood_soc2_summary.pdf (SOC 2 overview), support-access-flows.png (draft access flow).

Proposed next steps:
- You share quick answers inline below (2–3 bullets).
- We confirm whether Dedicated or Private deployment better matches residency/KMS requirements.
- Quick 30-min follow-up Wed or Thu next week to finalize the mutual action plan.

If you prefer, Monica can run a short tech workshop with your infra/security folks to walk the proposed break-glass flows.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415 555 0123


From: Elena Ramos <elena@clearline.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Clearline Security Team <security@clearline.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: RBAC & support access — post-demo checklist and clarifications

On Wed, Nov 18, 2026 at 2:12 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Elena,
> 
> Quick recap and clarifying questions we promised to follow up on...

Hi Ben — thanks for the summary. Answers below:

1) PII scope: primary items are account holder name, email, account number, last 4 of SSN, transaction metadata (amount, timestamp, merchant id). We also classify voice transcripts as PII when they include customer-identifying information.

2) Retention: regulatory requires we keep audit logs 7 years, raw inputs 2 years, and generated outputs 1 year unless a legal hold is in place. We are open to data minimization after 90 days for non-critical telemetry.

3) Residency: customer data must stay in the US. Some aggregated telemetry can be sent to EU for analytics, but logs and raw PII must remain in US regions.

4) Support access model: we run role tiers (Level 1 read-only, Level 2 with elevated troubleshooting access via JIT, Level 3 SRE/Admin). Any JIT elevation requires a ticket and a single manager approval. For production access to PII we require multi-person approval.

5) Break-glass: yes, emergency access must be approved by SRE lead + Security manager (two-person). We prefer approvals recorded in our ticketing system (Jira) or via DocuSign where appropriate.

6) Logging & evidence: logs must be retained 7 years and exported to our Splunk instance. We also need signed proof that KMS integrations support our HSM-backed keys.

I'll attach our internal support policy and a short security expectations doc. Happy to join the follow-up.

—Elena
Elena Ramos | Director of Security, Clearline Financial
elena@clearline.com | +1 617 555 4082


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Ramos <elena@clearline.com>, Clearline Security Team <security@clearline.com>
Date: 2026-11-19T09:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: RBAC & support access — post-demo checklist and clarifications

Internal — quick action list based on Elena's responses: please treat as high priority for RWI-2391.

Summary of key asks from Clearline:
- US-only residency for PII and logs.
- Audit log retention: 7 years, export to their Splunk.
- KMS must support HSM-backed keys / show integration path.
- Break-glass: two-person approval (SRE lead + Security manager), approval recorded in Jira or DocuSign.
- Retention windows: raw inputs 2y, outputs 1y (with legal hold exceptions).

Monica — can you:
- Draft a short support playbook that maps our role tiers to their Level 1/2/3, and note what we can provide for JIT (time-limited tokens, justifications in Jira).
- Produce a support-breakglass-draft.docx we can share with Clearline that includes the approval flow, audit trails, and who gets notified.

Vivek — can you:
- Confirm KMS/HSM integration options for private vs dedicated deployments and whether we can meet a 7-year log export requirement (storage + egress).
- Provide a short note we can share describing our logging retention policy and how we would push logs to their Splunk (format, S3 export, forwarder).

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Subject: RBAC & support access — post-demo checklist and clarifications

Hi Elena,

Thanks again for the demo call today — appreciate the frank conversation about how Clearline handles customer PII and incident response.

Quick recap and clarifying questions we promised to follow up on (answers will help scope implementation + any contract attachments):

1) PII scope: can you confirm the categories of data you consider PII for this workload (names, emails, SSNs, account numbers, transaction metadata, voice transcripts)?
2) Retention: target retention windows for raw inputs, generated outputs, and audit logs (what must be retained for compliance vs what can be auto-deleted)?
3) Residency: which geographic region(s) must data and logs reside in (US-only, EU-only, hybrid)?
4) Support access model: current least-privilege controls for support engineers (role tiers, just-in-time elevation, approval workflow)?
5) Break-glass: you mentioned a two-person approval for emergency access — can you confirm approvers are internal Clearline SRE + security manager, and whether approval can be via DocuSign or an internal ticketing workflow?
6) Logging & evidence: required log retention duration and whether logs must be exported to your SIEM (Splunk/Chronicle) or if Redwood-hosted logs are acceptable.

Attachments: mutual-action-plan.xlsx (proposed timeline + gating), redwood_soc2_summary.pdf (SOC 2 overview), support-access-flows.png (draft access flow).

Proposed next steps:
- You share quick answers inline below (2–3 bullets).
- We confirm whether Dedicated or Private deployment better matches residency/KMS requirements.
- Quick 30-min follow-up Wed or Thu next week to finalize the mutual action plan.

If you prefer, Monica can run a short tech workshop with your infra/security folks to walk the proposed break-glass flows.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415 555 0123


From: Elena Ramos <elena@clearline.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Clearline Security Team <security@clearline.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: RBAC & support access — post-demo checklist and clarifications

On Wed, Nov 18, 2026 at 2:12 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Elena,
> 
> Quick recap and clarifying questions we promised to follow up on...

Hi Ben — thanks for the summary. Answers below:

1) PII scope: primary items are account holder name, email, account number, last 4 of SSN, transaction metadata (amount, timestamp, merchant id). We also classify voice transcripts as PII when they include customer-identifying information.

2) Retention: regulatory requires we keep audit logs 7 years, raw inputs 2 years, and generated outputs 1 year unless a legal hold is in place. We are open to data minimization after 90 days for non-critical telemetry.

3) Residency: customer data must stay in the US. Some aggregated telemetry can be sent to EU for analytics, but logs and raw PII must remain in US regions.

4) Support access model: we run role tiers (Level 1 read-only, Level 2 with elevated troubleshooting access via JIT, Level 3 SRE/Admin). Any JIT elevation requires a ticket and a single manager approval. For production access to PII we require multi-person approval.

5) Break-glass: yes, emergency access must be approved by SRE lead + Security manager (two-person). We prefer approvals recorded in our ticketing system (Jira) or via DocuSign where appropriate.

6) Logging & evidence: logs must be retained 7 years and exported to our Splunk instance. We also need signed proof that KMS integrations support our HSM-backed keys.

I'll attach our internal support policy and a short security expectations doc. Happy to join the follow-up.

—Elena

elena@clearline.com | +1 617 555 4082

- Customer fix (their side): unknown, likely 2 sprints if they need partner changes

Recommendation: Approve a 30-day extension, but give 7k tokens instead of 10k and offer sandbox provisioning (we can have it ready in 24 hours). That reduces promo spend while keeping them moving for the demo. I’ve attached a proposed provisioning timeline (integration_timeline.pdf).

title: PrivDeploy: Day-0 prerequisites & ownership matrix
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Gibbons <alex.gibbons@medicore.com>, Medicore Health - Platform Team <platform@medicore.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: PrivDeploy: Day-0 prerequisites & ownership matrix

Alex / Platform team —

Following our kickoff yesterday, attached is a concise Day‑0 checklist I promised. Goal: remove ambiguity on who delivers each item so we don’t hit last‑minute gating issues when we handover the VPC peering and KMS access. High level asks:

- Please confirm the CIDR ranges you plan to reserve for the Redwood tenant (we need the /26 for control plane + /20 for worker subnets).
- Provide the egress allowlist IPs and proxy configuration for outbound calls to S3 and our telemetry endpoint.
- Share a short network diagram (peering, NAT, firewall tiers) and an initial list of service accounts you’ll use for the peering/JIT keys.

I’ve populated column ownership in the attached spreadsheet (priv-deploy-prereqs.xlsx) with our proposed split — review and flag anything you’d like us to take.

Next steps: if Medicore can confirm items 1–4 by EOD next Wed (11/18), we can schedule the Day‑0 smoke run for Fri (11/20). If any of those become blockers, call it out in the reply so we can escalate internally.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142
Redwood Inference

Attachments: priv-deploy-prereqs.xlsx (spreadsheet, 12KB), medicore-network-diagram-v1.png (image, 48KB)

From: Alex Gibbons <alex.gibbons@medicore.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Medicore Health - Platform Team <platform@medicore.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 11:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrivDeploy: Day-0 prerequisites & ownership matrix

Ben —

Thanks for the checklist and the clear owner matrix. Quick answers inline:

- CIDR ranges: we can reserve 10.40.0.0/20 for worker subnets and 10.40.64.0/26 for control plane; please confirm those don’t overlap with any other tenant blocks you’re planning.
- Egress: we’ll allow outbound to S3 (*.amazonaws.com) via our proxy at 34.201.120.10; for telemetry, we’ll permit 52.23.44.77/32 to your telemetry ingestion endpoint.
- Network diagram: attached an updated PNG with our NAT tier and the transit gateway connection.
- Service accounts: sending a separate CSV by EOD — platform@medicore will be the owner for peering and cross‑account roles.

One outstanding item: KMS integration. We need to confirm whether Redwood requires a customer master key in our account or if you will use an external KMS proxy. We’re leaning toward using our KMS (CMK in our org) for keys at rest — any constraints there?

If that’s the only open item, we can meet the 11/18 confirmation.

—Alex
Alex Gibbons
SRE Lead, Platform | Medicore Health
alex.gibbons@medicore.com

Attachment: medicore-network-diagram-v1.png

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Gibbons <alex.gibbons@medicore.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:36:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrivDeploy: Day-0 prerequisites & ownership matrix

Alex / team —

Thanks for the quick responses and the diagram. Short answers from the Redwood side:

- CIDR: 10.40.0.0/20 and 10.40.64.0/26 look fine; we’ll mark those as reserved in our infra catalog. Confirmed no overlap with current tenants.
- Egress: the IPs look good. We’ll add the proxy IP to our egress allowlist and ensure control plane health checks route via that proxy.
- KMS: Redwood supports both models. Two recommended options:
  1) Medicore-managed CMK in your account (preferred when you require complete key control). We’ll integrate by creating an IAM role for Redwood to use the key for disk/data encryption during provisioning.
  2) Redwood-hosted key proxy (we manage CEK wrapping, you supply HSM-backed secrets via a vault connector). This simplifies cross-account permissions but requires trust in our key proxy.

If Medicore requires full key ownership for compliance, go with option 1. We’ll prepare the IAM policy and a short runbook describing the minimal permissions we need.

Action items from Redwood (assigned):
- K1: Draft IAM policy + minimal privileges for CMK integration (owner: Karthik) — ETA 11/17 EOD.
- K2: Update internal infra catalog to reserve CIDR blocks (owner: Jonas) — ETA 11/16.
- B1: Prep Day‑0 smoke script and test vector for the environment activation (owner: Ben) — ETA 11/19.

I’ve attached a short runbook link and will push the IAM policy draft to the shared drive.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Inference Platform Integrations | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com

Related link: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a2b3c-priv-prereqs

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Gibbons <alex.gibbons@medicore.com>, Medicore Health - Platform Team <platform@medicore.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Nov 2026 17:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrivDeploy: Day-0 prerequisites & ownership matrix

Alex / Platform team —

Appreciate the confirmations and the diagram. Quick summary and next steps so we’re all aligned before the smoke run: 

- Medicore will deliver the service account CSV and confirm KMS choice by 11/18 EOD.
- Redwood will provide the IAM policy draft for CMK integration (Karthik) and the Day‑0 smoke script (Ben) by 11/19 EOD.
- Tentative smoke window: Fri 11/20 10:00–12:00 PT. Please reply if that conflicts with maintenance windows.

If we get the KMS decision late, we can still run the network/certificate/light provisioning checks during the smoke window and postpone full data‑at‑rest validation until KMS is resolved.

Thanks — looking forward to the day‑0 run.

Ben Carter
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments referenced: priv-deploy-prereqs.xlsx
